Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|lt=Naiku|lt_ja=内宮}} and {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮}}) * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡社}} * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社}} – Built during the {{ill|Bunsei era|ja|文政}} ({{ill|1818|ja|1818}} – {{ill|1831|ja|1831}}) * Sarutahiko Shrine, Tarika Shrine, and Kakimoto Shrine – Enshrining, in addition to {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦}}, {{ill|Yamazaki Ansai|ja|山崎闇斎}}, who advocated {{ill|Tarika Shintō|ja|垂加神道}}, and {{ill|Kakimoto no Hitomaro|ja|柿本人麻呂}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社}} * {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Munakata Shrine|lt_ja=宗像社}} * {{ill|Tenmangu Shrine|ja|天満宮}} – Enshrined deity: Kitano Ōkami * {{ill|Ōkuninushi Shrine|ja|大国主}} * Goshasōdensho – Saibe Shrine, {{ill|Dai-Shogun Shrine|ja|大将軍}} Shrine, Kochiho Shrine, {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社}}, and {{ill|Hiyoshisha|ja|日吉社}} * Kagura Hall * Palanquin Pavilion * Shrine Gate == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇}}’s Imperial Edict Prayer Document === Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto City === * Main Hall * Offerings Hall * Worship Hall * Corridor == Festivals == Until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}} the Mikoshi Reception was held on {{ill|July 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月18日 (旧暦)}} and the Goryo Festival on {{ill|August 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|8月18日 (旧暦)}}. After the Meiji period, May1 was designated as the Divine Auspice Festival (Shinkō‑sai) and the 18th as the Homecoming Festival (Kankō‑sai), with August18 established as the regular festival day on which a Tōyū performance is offered. Currently, the Homecoming Festival is held on either the third or fourth Sunday of May, and during the evening festival a children’s mikoshi is organized in collaboration with local elementary schools. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Goryo Faith|ja|御霊信仰}} * {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimogoryo-jinja}} * [http://shimogoryo.main.jp/ Shimogoryo Shrine Official Website] {{Kyōyō Tenmangu Twenty-Five Shrine Pilgrimage}} {{Shinto Portal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Independent Shrines]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of the Twenty-Five Shrines of Kyōyō Tenmangu]] [[Category:Important Scenic Buildings of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Emperor Reigen]] [[Category:Translated Shrines and Temples]] [[Category:Shimogoryo Shrine]] [[Category:Goryo Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category: tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimo-Goryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimo-Goryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimo-Goryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:下御霊神社]] [[Category:New]]

{{infobox religious building | name = Shimogoryo Shrine | native_name = 下御霊神社 | address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}{{ill|Nakagyo Ward|ja|中京区}}{{ill|Teramachi Street|ja|寺町通}}{{ill|Marutamachi Street|ja|丸太町通|lt=Marutamachi|lt_ja=丸太町}} 下る下御霊前町 | coordinates = {{coord|35|01|01|N|135|46|04|E|region:JP-26|type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shimogoryo Shrine}} | deities = 吉備聖霊
{{ill|Prince Hayara|ja|早良親王|lt=Emperor Suidō|lt_ja=崇道天皇}}
{{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王}}
{{ill|Fujiwara Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|lt=Lady Fujiwara|lt_ja=藤原大夫人}}
{{ill|Fujiwara Hirotsugu|ja|藤原広嗣|lt=Fujiwara Daitō|lt_ja=藤大夫}}
{{ill|Tachibana Itsusei|ja|橘逸勢|lt=Tachibana Daifu|lt_ja=橘大夫}}
{{ill|Fumimuro no Miyata no Maro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|lt=Fumi Daifu|lt_ja=文大夫}}
火雷天神 | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|Jōgan}}5年（{{ill|863|ja|863}}） | status = 旧{{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社}} | pilgrimage = {{ill|Kyoto-Rakuya Hachasha Shūin Meguri|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり}} | festival = 5月第3日曜日（御霊祭） | File = [[File:Shimogoryo_torii.jpg|250px|]] }} '''Shimogoryo Shrine''' (下御霊神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}{{ill|Nakagyo Ward|ja|中京区}}, Shimogoryo‑mae Town. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社}}. The shrine’s name corresponds with that of {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社}}. == History == During the era of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}}, epidemics spread throughout various regions. This was regarded as a curse ({{ill|Curse|ja|祟り}}) stemming from the {{ill|Goryo Faith|ja|御霊信仰|lt=Spirits|lt_ja=御霊}}, and accordingly, on May 20 of {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|Jōgan}}5 ({{ill|863|ja|863}}), a Goryo Gathering ({{ill|Goryo Gathering|ja|御霊会}}) was held at the {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京}} site of {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑}}. This gathering is considered to mark the founding of the enshrinements at both this shrine and {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社}}. Since ancient times, it has been revered as the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial Palace|ja|京都御所}}. Originally, the deity was enshrined in the Goryo Hall of Shimodashimori‑ji (下出雲寺御霊堂) located in the Izumo District of Atago County ({{ill|Atago District|ja|愛宕郡}}, {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郷}}) on the route of {{ill|Izumo Road|ja|出雲路}}. To the north stood {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社}}, known as the Goryo Hall of Kamidashimori‑ji. Later, the shrine was relocated to Shinmachi Izumi, and subsequently, in Tenshō18 ({{ill|1590|ja|1590}}), as part of the urban development initiated by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, it was moved to the site where {{ill|Saionji Sanjitsu|ja|西園寺実氏}} had his villa, Tokiwaido. During the Kyōhō era ({{ill|1716|ja|1716}} – {{ill|1736|ja|1736}}), the spirit of {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇}} was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in Kansei3 ({{ill|1791|ja|1791}}) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence, after the old building was destroyed in the Great Tenmei Fire of Tenmei8 ({{ill|1788|ja|1788}}). In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}, it was categorized as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社}}. Notably, scholar {{ill|Shiga Tsuyoshi|ja|志賀剛}} of Shikinai shrines has identified this shrine with {{ill|Izumo Iokami Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社}} (Shikinai Taisha) as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} The enshrined deities consist of the following eight gods, collectively known as the "Eight Goryo": * Kibi Seirei (although often identified with {{ill|Kibi Mabibi|ja|吉備真備}}, since Kibi Mabibi did not die in a state of indignation, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|Japanese spirit|ja|和魂}} of the six divine spirits) * Emperor Suidō (the crown prince of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}}, {{ill|Prince Hayara|ja|早良親王}}) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}}) * Lady Fujiwara (mother of Prince Iyo, {{ill|Fujiwara Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子}}) * Fujiwara Daitō ({{ill|Fujiwara Hirotsugu|ja|藤原広嗣}}) * Tachibana Daifu ({{ill|Tachibana Itsusei|ja|橘逸勢}}) * Fumi Daifu ({{ill|Fumimuro no Miyata no Maro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|lt=Fumi Daifu|lt_ja=文屋宮田麻呂}}) * Karei Tenjin (although often identified with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}, since the shrine was founded before he came to be regarded as Tenjin, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|rough spirit|ja|荒魂}} of the six divine spirits) All these deities are individuals who met their untimely deaths amid political strife, and this shrine was established to appease their vengeful spirits ({{ill|Vengeful Spirit|ja|怨霊}}). In the side hall, {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇}} is enshrined as the Tennchūbashira Imperial Deity, which is attributed to the close ties between the shrine’s then priests, the father and son {{ill|Izumori Nobu-nao|ja|出雲路信直}} and {{ill|Izumori Naomoto|ja|出雲路直元}}, and the emperor. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) – Reconstructed in Kansei3 ({{ill|1791|ja|1791}}) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence. * Offerings Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Corridor (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Worship Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Prayer Hall * Shrine Office * Shinmeisha ({{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|lt=Naiku|lt_ja=内宮}} and {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮}}) * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡社}} * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社}} – Built during the {{ill|Bunsei era|ja|文政}} ({{ill|1818|ja|1818}} – {{ill|1831|ja|1831}}) * Sarutahiko Shrine, Tarika Shrine, and Kakimoto Shrine – Enshrining, in addition to {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦}}, {{ill|Yamazaki Ansai|ja|山崎闇斎}}, who advocated {{ill|Tarika Shintō|ja|垂加神道}}, and {{ill|Kakimoto no Hitomaro|ja|柿本人麻呂}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社}} * {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Munakata Shrine|lt_ja=宗像社}} * {{ill|Tenmangu Shrine|ja|天満宮}} – Enshrined deity: Kitano Ōkami * {{ill|Ōkuninushi Shrine|ja|大国主}} * Goshasōdensho – Saibe Shrine, {{ill|Dai-Shogun Shrine|ja|大将軍}} Shrine, Kochiho Shrine, {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社}}, and {{ill|Hiyoshisha|ja|日吉社}} * Kagura Hall * Palanquin Pavilion * Shrine Gate == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇}}’s Imperial Edict Prayer Document === Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto City === * Main Hall * Offerings Hall * Worship Hall * Corridor == Festivals == Until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}} the Mikoshi Reception was held on {{ill|July 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月18日 (旧暦)}} and the Goryo Festival on {{ill|August 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|8月18日 (旧暦)}}. After the Meiji period, May1 was designated as the Divine Auspice Festival (Shinkō‑sai) and the 18th as the Homecoming Festival (Kankō‑sai), with August18 established as the regular festival day on which a Tōyū performance is offered. Currently, the Homecoming Festival is held on either the third or fourth Sunday of May, and during the evening festival a children’s mikoshi is organized in collaboration with local elementary schools. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{Reflist}}== See Also == * {{ill|Goryo Faith|ja|御霊信仰}} * {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimogoryo-jinja}} * [http://shimogoryo.main.jp/ Shimogoryo Shrine Official Website] {{Kyōyō Tenmangu Twenty-Five Shrine Pilgrimage}} {{Shinto Portal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Independent Shrines]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of the Twenty-Five Shrines of Kyōyō Tenmangu]] [[Category:Important Scenic Buildings of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Emperor Reigen]] [[Category:Translated Shrines and Temples]] [[Category:Shimogoryo Shrine]] [[Category:Goryo Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category: tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimo-Goryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimo-Goryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimo-Goryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:下御霊神社]] [[Category:New]]

{{ill|Fujiwara Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|lt=Lady Fujiwara|lt_ja=藤原大夫人}}
{{ill|Fujiwara Hirotsugu|ja|藤原広嗣|lt=Fujiwara Daitō|lt_ja=藤大夫}}
{{ill|Tachibana Itsusei|ja|橘逸勢|lt=Tachibana Daifu|lt_ja=橘大夫}}
{{ill|Fumimuro no Miyata no Maro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|lt=Fumi Daifu|lt_ja=文大夫}}
火雷天神 | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|Jōgan}}5年（{{ill|863|ja|863}}） | status = 旧{{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社}} | pilgrimage = {{ill|Kyoto-Rakuya Hachasha Shūin Meguri|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり}} | festival = 5月第3日曜日（御霊祭） | File = [[File:Shimogoryo_torii.jpg|250px|]] }} '''Shimogoryo Shrine''' (下御霊神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}{{ill|Nakagyo Ward|ja|中京区}}, Shimogoryo‑mae Town. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社}}. The shrine’s name corresponds with that of {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社}}. == History == During the era of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}}, epidemics spread throughout various regions. This was regarded as a curse ({{ill|Curse|ja|祟り}}) stemming from the {{ill|Goryo Faith|ja|御霊信仰|lt=Spirits|lt_ja=御霊}}, and accordingly, on May 20 of {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|Jōgan}}5 ({{ill|863|ja|863}}), a Goryo Gathering ({{ill|Goryo Gathering|ja|御霊会}}) was held at the {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京}} site of {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑}}. This gathering is considered to mark the founding of the enshrinements at both this shrine and {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社}}. Since ancient times, it has been revered as the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial Palace|ja|京都御所}}. Originally, the deity was enshrined in the Goryo Hall of Shimodashimori‑ji (下出雲寺御霊堂) located in the Izumo District of Atago County ({{ill|Atago District|ja|愛宕郡}}, {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郷}}) on the route of {{ill|Izumo Road|ja|出雲路}}. To the north stood {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社}}, known as the Goryo Hall of Kamidashimori‑ji. Later, the shrine was relocated to Shinmachi Izumi, and subsequently, in Tenshō18 ({{ill|1590|ja|1590}}), as part of the urban development initiated by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, it was moved to the site where {{ill|Saionji Sanjitsu|ja|西園寺実氏}} had his villa, Tokiwaido. During the Kyōhō era ({{ill|1716|ja|1716}} – {{ill|1736|ja|1736}}), the spirit of {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇}} was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in Kansei3 ({{ill|1791|ja|1791}}) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence, after the old building was destroyed in the Great Tenmei Fire of Tenmei8 ({{ill|1788|ja|1788}}). In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}, it was categorized as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社}}. Notably, scholar {{ill|Shiga Tsuyoshi|ja|志賀剛}} of Shikinai shrines has identified this shrine with {{ill|Izumo Iokami Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社}} (Shikinai Taisha) as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} The enshrined deities consist of the following eight gods, collectively known as the "Eight Goryo": * Kibi Seirei (although often identified with {{ill|Kibi Mabibi|ja|吉備真備}}, since Kibi Mabibi did not die in a state of indignation, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|Japanese spirit|ja|和魂}} of the six divine spirits) * Emperor Suidō (the crown prince of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}}, {{ill|Prince Hayara|ja|早良親王}}) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}}) * Lady Fujiwara (mother of Prince Iyo, {{ill|Fujiwara Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子}}) * Fujiwara Daitō ({{ill|Fujiwara Hirotsugu|ja|藤原広嗣}}) * Tachibana Daifu ({{ill|Tachibana Itsusei|ja|橘逸勢}}) * Fumi Daifu ({{ill|Fumimuro no Miyata no Maro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|lt=Fumi Daifu|lt_ja=文屋宮田麻呂}}) * Karei Tenjin (although often identified with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}, since the shrine was founded before he came to be regarded as Tenjin, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|rough spirit|ja|荒魂}} of the six divine spirits) All these deities are individuals who met their untimely deaths amid political strife, and this shrine was established to appease their vengeful spirits ({{ill|Vengeful Spirit|ja|怨霊}}). In the side hall, {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇}} is enshrined as the Tennchūbashira Imperial Deity, which is attributed to the close ties between the shrine’s then priests, the father and son {{ill|Izumori Nobu-nao|ja|出雲路信直}} and {{ill|Izumori Naomoto|ja|出雲路直元}}, and the emperor. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) – Reconstructed in Kansei3 ({{ill|1791|ja|1791}}) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence. * Offerings Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Corridor (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Worship Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Prayer Hall * Shrine Office * Shinmeisha ({{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|lt=Naiku|lt_ja=内宮}} and {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮}}) * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡社}} * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社}} – Built during the {{ill|Bunsei era|ja|文政}} ({{ill|1818|ja|1818}} – {{ill|1831|ja|1831}}) * Sarutahiko Shrine, Tarika Shrine, and Kakimoto Shrine – Enshrining, in addition to {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦}}, {{ill|Yamazaki Ansai|ja|山崎闇斎}}, who advocated {{ill|Tarika Shintō|ja|垂加神道}}, and {{ill|Kakimoto no Hitomaro|ja|柿本人麻呂}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社}} * {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Munakata Shrine|lt_ja=宗像社}} * {{ill|Tenmangu Shrine|ja|天満宮}} – Enshrined deity: Kitano Ōkami * {{ill|Ōkuninushi Shrine|ja|大国主}} * Goshasōdensho – Saibe Shrine, {{ill|Dai-Shogun Shrine|ja|大将軍}} Shrine, Kochiho Shrine, {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社}}, and {{ill|Hiyoshisha|ja|日吉社}} * Kagura Hall * Palanquin Pavilion * Shrine Gate == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇}}’s Imperial Edict Prayer Document === Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto City === * Main Hall * Offerings Hall * Worship Hall * Corridor == Festivals == Until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}} the Mikoshi Reception was held on {{ill|July 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月18日 (旧暦)}} and the Goryo Festival on {{ill|August 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|8月18日 (旧暦)}}. After the Meiji period, May1 was designated as the Divine Auspice Festival (Shinkō‑sai) and the 18th as the Homecoming Festival (Kankō‑sai), with August18 established as the regular festival day on which a Tōyū performance is offered. Currently, the Homecoming Festival is held on either the third or fourth Sunday of May, and during the evening festival a children’s mikoshi is organized in collaboration with local elementary schools. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also! == * {{ill|Goryo Faith|ja|御霊信仰}} * {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimogoryo-jinja}} * [http://shimogoryo.main.jp/ Shimogoryo Shrine Official Website] {{Kyōyō Tenmangu Twenty-Five Shrine Pilgrimage}} {{Shinto Portal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Independent Shrines]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of the Twenty-Five Shrines of Kyōyō Tenmangu]] [[Category:Important Scenic Buildings of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Emperor Reigen]] [[Category:Translated Shrines and Temples]] [[Category:Shimogoryo Shrine]] [[Category:Goryo Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category: tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimo-Goryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimo-Goryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimo-Goryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:下御霊神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

Notably, scholar {{ill|Shiga Tsuyoshi|ja|志賀剛}} of Shikinai shrines has identified this shrine with {{ill|Izumo Iokami Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社}} (Shikinai Taisha) as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} The enshrined deities consist of the following eight gods, collectively known as the "Eight Goryo": * Kibi Seirei (although often identified with {{ill|Kibi Mabibi|ja|吉備真備}}, since Kibi Mabibi did not die in a state of indignation, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|Japanese spirit|ja|和魂}} of the six divine spirits) * Emperor Suidō (the crown prince of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}}, {{ill|Prince Hayara|ja|早良親王}}) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}}) * Lady Fujiwara (mother of Prince Iyo, {{ill|Fujiwara Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子}}) * Fujiwara Daitō ({{ill|Fujiwara Hirotsugu|ja|藤原広嗣}}) * Tachibana Daifu ({{ill|Tachibana Itsusei|ja|橘逸勢}}) * Fumi Daifu ({{ill|Fumimuro no Miyata no Maro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|lt=Fumi Daifu|lt_ja=文屋宮田麻呂}}) * Karei Tenjin (although often identified with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}, since the shrine was founded before he came to be regarded as Tenjin, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|rough spirit|ja|荒魂}} of the six divine spirits) All these deities are individuals who met their untimely deaths amid political strife, and this shrine was established to appease their vengeful spirits ({{ill|Vengeful Spirit|ja|怨霊}}). In the side hall, {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇}} is enshrined as the Tennchūbashira Imperial Deity, which is attributed to the close ties between the shrine’s then priests, the father and son {{ill|Izumori Nobu-nao|ja|出雲路信直}} and {{ill|Izumori Naomoto|ja|出雲路直元}}, and the emperor. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) – Reconstructed in Corridor (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Worship Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Prayer Hall * Shrine Office * Shinmeisha ({{ill|Inner Document === Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto City === * Main Hall * Offerings Hall * Worship Hall * Corridor == Festivals == Until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}} the Mikoshi Reception was held on {{ill|July 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月18日 (旧暦)}} and the Goryo Festival on {{ill|August 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|8月18日 (旧暦)}}. After the Meiji period, May1 was designated as the Divine Auspice Festival (Shinkō‑sai) and the 18th as the Homecoming Festival (Kankō‑sai), with August18 established as the regular festival day on which a Tōyū performance is offered. Currently, the Homecoming Festival is held on either the third or fourth Sunday of May, and during the evening festival a children’s mikoshi is organized in collaboration with local elementary schools. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Goryo Faith|ja|御霊信仰}} * {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimogoryo-jinja}} * [http://shimogoryo.main.jp/ Shimogoryo Shrine Official Website] {{Kyōyō Tenmangu Twenty-Five Shrine Pilgrimage}} {{Shinto Portal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Independent Shrines]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of the Twenty-Five Shrines of Kyōyō Tenmangu]] [[Category:Important Scenic Buildings of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Emperor Reigen]] [[Category:Translated Shrines and Temples]] [[Category:Shimogoryo Shrine]] [[Category:Goryo Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category: tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimo-Goryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimo-Goryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimo-Goryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:下御霊神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

{{ill|Prince Hayara|ja|早良親王|lt=Emperor Suidō|lt_ja=崇道天皇}}
{{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王}}
{{ill|Fujiwara Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|lt=Lady Fujiwara|lt_ja=藤原大夫人}}
{{ill|Fujiwara Hirotsugu|ja|藤原広嗣|lt=Fujiwara Daitō|lt_ja=藤大夫}}
{{ill|Tachibana Itsusei|ja|橘逸勢|lt=Tachibana Daifu|lt_ja=橘大夫}}
{{ill|Fumimuro no Miyata no Maro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|lt=Fumi Daifu|lt_ja=文大夫}}
火雷天神 | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|Jōgan}}5th year （{{ill|863|ja|863}}） | status = 旧{{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社}} | pilgrimage = {{ill|Kyoto-Rakuya Hachasha Shūin Meguri|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり}} | festival = 5月第3日曜日（御霊祭） | File = [[File:Shimogoryo_torii.jpg|250px|]] }} '''Shimogoryo Shrine''' (下御霊神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}{{ill|Nakagyo Ward|ja|中京区}}, Shimogoryo‑mae Town. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社}}. The shrine’s name corresponds with that of {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社}}. == History == During the era of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}}, epidemics spread throughout various regions. This was regarded as a curse ({{ill|Curse|ja|祟り}}) stemming from the {{ill|Goryo Faith|ja|御霊信仰|lt=Spirits|lt_ja=御霊}}, and accordingly, on May 20 of {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|Jōgan}}5 ({{ill|863|ja|863}}), a Goryo Gathering ({{ill|Goryo Gathering|ja|御霊会}}) was held at the {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京}} site of {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑}}. This gathering is considered to mark the founding of the enshrinements at both this shrine and {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社}}. Since ancient times, it has been revered as the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial Palace|ja|京都御所}}. Originally, the deity was enshrined in the Goryo Hall of Shimodashimori‑ji (下出雲寺御霊堂) located in the Izumo District of Atago County ({{ill|Atago District|ja|愛宕郡}}, {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郷}}) on the route of {{ill|Izumo Road|ja|出雲路}}. To the north stood {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社}}, known as the Goryo Hall of Kamidashimori‑ji. Later, the shrine was relocated to Shinmachi Izumi, and subsequently, in Tenshō18 ({{ill|1590|ja|1590}}), as part of the urban development initiated by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, it was moved to the site where {{ill|Saionji Sanjitsu|ja|西園寺実氏}} had his villa, Tokiwaido. During the Kyōhō era ({{ill|1716|ja|1716}} – {{ill|1736|ja|1736}}), the spirit of {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇}} was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in Kansei3 ({{ill|1791|ja|1791}}) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence, after the old building was destroyed in the Great Tenmei Fire of Tenmei8 ({{ill|1788|ja|1788}}). In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}, it was categorized as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社}}. Notably, scholar {{ill|Shiga Tsuyoshi|ja|志賀剛}} of Shikinai shrines has identified this shrine with {{ill|Izumo Iokami Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社}} (Shikinai Taisha) as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} The enshrined deities consist of the following eight gods, collectively known as the "Eight Goryo": * Kibi Seirei (although often identified with {{ill|Kibi Mabibi|ja|吉備真備}}, since Kibi Mabibi did not die in a state of indignation, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|Japanese spirit|ja|和魂}} of the six divine spirits) * Emperor Suidō (the crown prince of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}}, {{ill|Prince Hayara|ja|早良親王}}) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}}) * Lady Fujiwara (mother of Prince Iyo, {{ill|Fujiwara Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子}}) * Fujiwara Daitō ({{ill|Fujiwara Hirotsugu|ja|藤原広嗣}}) * Tachibana Daifu ({{ill|Tachibana Itsusei|ja|橘逸勢}}) * Fumi Daifu ({{ill|Fumimuro no Miyata no Maro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|lt=Fumi Daifu|lt_ja=文屋宮田麻呂}}) * Karei Tenjin (although often identified with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}, since the shrine was founded before he came to be regarded as Tenjin, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|rough spirit|ja|荒魂}} of the six divine spirits) All these deities are individuals who met their untimely deaths amid political strife, and this shrine was established to appease their vengeful spirits ({{ill|Vengeful Spirit|ja|怨霊}}). In the side hall, {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇}} is enshrined as the Tennchūbashira Imperial Deity, which is attributed to the close ties between the shrine’s then priests, the father and son {{ill|Izumori Nobu-nao|ja|出雲路信直}} and {{ill|Izumori Naomoto|ja|出雲路直元}}, and the emperor. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) – Reconstructed in Kansei3 ({{ill|1791|ja|1791}}) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence. * Offerings Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Currently, the Homecoming Festival is held on either the third or fourth Sunday of May, and during the evening festival a children’s mikoshi is organized in collaboration with local elementary schools. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Goryo Faith|ja|御霊信仰}} * {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimogoryo-jinja}} * [http://shimogoryo.main.jp/ Shimogoryo Shrine Official Website] {{Kyōyō Tenmangu Twenty-Five Shrine Pilgrimage}} {{Shinto Portal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Independent Shrines]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of the Twenty-Five Shrines of Kyōyō Tenmangu]] [[Category:Important Scenic Buildings of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Emperor Reigen]] [[Category:Translated Shrines and Temples]] [[Category:Shimogoryo Shrine]] [[Category:Goryo Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimo-Goryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimo-Goryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimo-Goryō Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:下御霊神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{infobox religious building | name = Shimogoryo Shrine | native_name = 下御霊神社 | address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}{{ill|Nakagyo Ward|ja|中京区}}{{ill|Teramachi Street|ja|寺町通}}{{ill|Marutamachi Street|ja|丸太町通|lt=Marutamachi|lt_ja=丸太町}} 下る下御霊前町 | coordinates = {{coord|35|01|01|N|135|46|04|E|region:JP-26|type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shimogoryo Shrine}} | deities = 吉備聖霊
{{ill|Prince Hayara|ja|早良親王|lt=Emperor Suidō|lt_ja=崇道天皇}}
{{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王}}
{{ill|Fujiwara Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|lt=Lady Fujiwara|lt_ja=藤原大夫人}}
{{ill|Fujiwara Hirotsugu|ja|藤原広嗣|lt=Fujiwara Daitō|lt_ja=藤大夫}}
{{ill|Tachibana Itsusei|ja|橘逸勢|lt=Tachibana Daifu|lt_ja=橘大夫}}
{{ill|Fumimuro no Miyata no Maro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|lt=Fumi Daifu|lt_ja=文大夫}}
火雷天神 | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|Jōgan}}5th year （{{ill|863|ja|863}}） | status = 旧{{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社}} | pilgrimage = {{ill|Kyoto-Rakuya Hachasha Shūin Meguri|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり}} | festival = 5月第3日曜日（御霊祭） | File = [[File:Shimogoryo_torii.jpg|250px|]] }} '''Shimogoryo Shrine''' (下御霊神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}{{ill|Nakagyo Ward|ja|中京区}}, Shimogoryo‑mae Town. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社}}. The shrine’s name corresponds with that of {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社}}. == History == During the era of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}}, epidemics spread throughout various regions. This was regarded as a curse ({{ill|Curse|ja|祟り}}) stemming from the {{ill|Goryo Faith|ja|御霊信仰|lt=Spirits|lt_ja=御霊}}, and accordingly, on May 20 of {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|Jōgan}}5 ({{ill|863|ja|863}}), a Goryo Gathering ({{ill|Goryo Gathering|ja|御霊会}}) was held at the {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京}} site of {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑}}. This gathering is considered to mark the founding of the enshrinements at both this shrine and {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社}}. Since ancient times, it has been revered as the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial Palace|ja|京都御所}}. Originally, the deity was enshrined in the Goryo Hall of Shimodashimori‑ji (下出雲寺御霊堂) located in the Izumo District of Atago County ({{ill|Atago District|ja|愛宕郡}}, {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郷}}) on the route of {{ill|Izumo Road|ja|出雲路}}. To the north stood {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社}}, known as the Goryo Hall of Kamidashimori‑ji. Later, the shrine was relocated to Shinmachi Izumi, and subsequently, in Tenshō18 ({{ill|1590|ja|1590}}), as part of the urban development initiated by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, it was moved to the site where {{ill|Saionji Sanjitsu|ja|西園寺実氏}} had his villa, Tokiwaido. During the Kyōhō era ({{ill|1716|ja|1716}} – {{ill|1736|ja|1736}}), the spirit of {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇}} was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in Kansei3 ({{ill|1791|ja|1791}}) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence, after the old building was destroyed in the Great Tenmei Fire of Tenmei8 ({{ill|1788|ja|1788}}). In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}, it was categorized as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社}}. Notably, scholar {{ill|Shiga Tsuyoshi|ja|志賀剛}} of Shikinai shrines has identified this shrine with {{ill|Izumo Iokami Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社}} (Shikinai Taisha) as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} The enshrined deities consist of the following eight gods, collectively known as the "Eight Goryo": * Kibi Seirei (although often identified with {{ill|Kibi Mabibi|ja|吉備真備}}, since Kibi Mabibi did not die in a state of indignation, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|Japanese spirit|ja|和魂}} of the six divine spirits) * Emperor Suidō (the crown prince of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}}, {{ill|Prince Hayara|ja|早良親王}}) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}}) * Lady Fujiwara (mother of Prince Iyo, {{ill|Fujiwara Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子}}) * Fujiwara Daitō ({{ill|Fujiwara Hirotsugu|ja|藤原広嗣}}) * Tachibana Daifu ({{ill|Tachibana Itsusei|ja|橘逸勢}}) * Fumi Daifu ({{ill|Fumimuro no Miyata no Maro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|lt=Fumi Daifu|lt_ja=文屋宮田麻呂}}) * Karei Tenjin (although often identified with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}, since the shrine was founded before he came to be regarded as Tenjin, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|rough spirit|ja|荒魂}} of the six divine spirits) All these deities are individuals who met their untimely deaths amid political strife, and this shrine was established to appease their vengeful spirits ({{ill|Vengeful Spirit|ja|怨霊}}). In the side hall, {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇}} is enshrined as the Tennchūbashira Imperial Deity, which is attributed to the close ties between the shrine’s then priests, the father and son {{ill|Izumori Nobu-nao|ja|出雲路信直}} and {{ill|Izumori Naomoto|ja|出雲路直元}}, and the emperor. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) – Reconstructed in Kansei3 ({{ill|1791|ja|1791}}) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence. * Offerings Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Corridor (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Worship Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Prayer Hall * Shrine Office * Shinmeisha ({{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|lt=Naiku|lt_ja=内宮}} and {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮}}) * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡社}} * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社}} – Built during the {{ill|Bunsei era|ja|文政}} ({{ill|1818|ja|1818}} – {{ill|1831|ja|1831}}) * Sarutahiko Shrine, Tarika Shrine, and Kakimoto Shrine – Enshrining, in addition to {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦}}, {{ill|Yamazaki Ansai|ja|山崎闇斎}}, who advocated {{ill|Tarika Shintō|ja|垂加神道}}, and {{ill|Kakimoto no Hitomaro|ja|柿本人麻呂}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社}} * {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Munakata Shrine|lt_ja=宗像社}} * {{ill|Tenmangu Shrine|ja|天満宮}} – Enshrined deity: Kitano Ōkami * {{ill|Ōkuninushi Shrine|ja|大国主}} * Goshasōdensho – Saibe Shrine, {{ill|Dai-Shogun Shrine|ja|大将軍}} Shrine, Kochiho Shrine, {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社}}, and {{ill|Hiyoshisha|ja|日吉社}} * Kagura Hall * Palanquin Pavilion * Shrine Gate == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇}}’s Imperial Edict Prayer Document === Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto City === * Main Hall * Offerings Hall * Worship Hall * Corridor == Festivals == Until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}} the Mikoshi Reception was held on {{ill|July 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月18日 (旧暦)}} and the Goryo Festival on {{ill|August 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|8月18日 (旧暦)}}. After the Meiji period, May1 was designated as the Divine Auspice Festival (Shinkō‑sai) and the 18th as the Homecoming Festival (Kankō‑sai), with August18 established as the regular festival day on which a Tōyū performance is offered. Currently, the Homecoming Festival is held on either the third or fourth Sunday of May, and during the evening festival a children’s mikoshi is organized in collaboration with local elementary schools. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Goryo Faith|ja|御霊信仰}} * {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimogoryo-jinja}} * [http://shimogoryo.main.jp/ Shimogoryo Shrine Official Website] {{Kyōyō Tenmangu Twenty-Five Shrine Pilgrimage}} {{Shinto Portal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Independent Shrines]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of the Twenty-Five Shrines of Kyōyō Tenmangu]] [[Category:Important Scenic Buildings of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Emperor Reigen]] [[Category:Translated Shrines and Temples]] [[Category:Shimogoryo Shrine]] [[Category:Goryo Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Shimo-Goryō Shrine]] [[ja:下御霊神社]]

火雷天神 | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|Jōgan|12=draft}}5th year （{{ill|863|ja|863|12=draft}}） | status = 旧{{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|12=draft}} | pilgrimage = {{ill|Kyoto-Rakuya Hachasha Shūin Meguri|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|12=draft}} | festival = 5月第3日曜日（御霊祭） | File = [[File:Shimogoryo_torii.jpg|250px|]] }} '''Shimogoryo Shrine''' (下御霊神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|12=draft}}{{ill|Nakagyo Ward|ja|中京区|12=draft}}, Shimogoryo‑mae Town. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|12=draft}}. The shrine’s name corresponds with that of {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=draft}}. == History == During the era of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft}}, epidemics spread throughout various regions. This was regarded as a curse ({{ill|Curse|ja|祟り|12=draft}}) stemming from the {{ill|Goryo Faith|ja|御霊信仰|lt=Spirits|lt_ja=御霊|12=draft}}, and accordingly, on May 20 of {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|Jōgan|12=draft}}5 ({{ill|863|ja|863|12=draft}}), a Goryo Gathering ({{ill|Goryo Gathering|ja|御霊会|12=draft}}) was held at the {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京|12=draft}} site of {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑|12=draft}}. This gathering is considered to mark the founding of the enshrinements at both this shrine and {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=draft}}. Since ancient times, it has been revered as the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial Palace|ja|京都御所|12=draft}}. Originally, the deity was enshrined in the Goryo Hall of Shimodashimori‑ji (下出雲寺御霊堂) located in the Izumo District of Atago County ({{ill|Atago District|ja|愛宕郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郷|12=draft}}) on the route of {{ill|Izumo Road|ja|出雲路|12=draft}}. To the north stood {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=draft}}, known as the Goryo Hall of Kamidashimori‑ji. Later, the shrine was relocated to Shinmachi Izumi, and subsequently, in Tenshō18 ({{ill|1590|ja|1590|12=draft}}), as part of the urban development initiated by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|12=draft}}, it was moved to the site where {{ill|Saionji Sanjitsu|ja|西園寺実氏|12=draft}} had his villa, Tokiwaido. During the Kyōhō era ({{ill|1716|ja|1716|12=draft}} – {{ill|1736|ja|1736|12=draft}}), the spirit of {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|12=draft}} was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in Kansei3 ({{ill|1791|ja|1791|12=draft}}) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence, after the old building was destroyed in the Great Tenmei Fire of Tenmei8 ({{ill|1788|ja|1788|12=draft}}). In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|12=draft}}, it was categorized as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|12=draft}}. Notably, scholar {{ill|Shiga Tsuyoshi|ja|志賀剛|12=draft}} of Shikinai shrines has identified this shrine with {{ill|Izumo Iokami Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|12=draft}} (Shikinai Taisha) as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=draft}}}} The enshrined deities consist of the following eight gods, collectively known as the "Eight Goryo": * Kibi Seirei (although often identified with {{ill|Kibi Mabibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft}}, since Kibi Mabibi did not die in a state of indignation, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|Japanese spirit|ja|和魂|12=draft}} of the six divine spirits) * Emperor Suidō (the crown prince of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft}}, {{ill|Prince Hayara|ja|早良親王|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|12=draft}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft}}) * Lady Fujiwara (mother of Prince Iyo, {{ill|Fujiwara Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=draft}}) * Fujiwara Daitō ({{ill|Fujiwara Hirotsugu|ja|藤原広嗣|12=draft}}) * Tachibana Daifu ({{ill|Tachibana Itsusei|ja|橘逸勢|12=draft}}) * Fumi Daifu ({{ill|Fumimuro no Miyata no Maro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|lt=Fumi Daifu|lt_ja=文屋宮田麻呂|12=draft}}) * Karei Tenjin (although often identified with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}}, since the shrine was founded before he came to be regarded as Tenjin, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|rough spirit|ja|荒魂|12=draft}} of the six divine spirits) All these deities are individuals who met their untimely deaths amid political strife, and this shrine was established to appease their vengeful spirits ({{ill|Vengeful Spirit|ja|怨霊|12=draft}}). In the side hall, {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|12=draft}} is enshrined as the Tennchūbashira Imperial Deity, which is attributed to the close ties between the shrine’s then priests, the father and son {{ill|Izumori Nobu-nao|ja|出雲路信直|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izumori Naomoto|ja|出雲路直元|12=draft}}, and the emperor. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) – Reconstructed in Kansei3 ({{ill|1791|ja|1791|12=draft}}) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence. * Offerings Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Corridor (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Worship Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Prayer Hall * Shrine Office * Shinmeisha ({{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|lt=Naiku|lt_ja=内宮|12=draft}} and {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社|12=draft}} – Built during the {{ill|Bunsei era|ja|文政|12=draft}} ({{ill|1818|ja|1818|12=draft}} – {{ill|1831|ja|1831|12=draft}}) * Sarutahiko Shrine, Tarika Shrine, and Kakimoto Shrine – Enshrining, in addition to {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamazaki Ansai|ja|山崎闇斎|12=draft}}, who advocated {{ill|Tarika Shintō|ja|垂加神道|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kakimoto no Hitomaro|ja|柿本人麻呂|12=draft}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Munakata Shrine|lt_ja=宗像社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tenmangu Shrine|ja|天満宮|12=draft}} – Enshrined deity: Kitano Ōkami * {{ill|Ōkuninushi Shrine|ja|大国主|12=draft}} * Goshasōdensho – Saibe Shrine, {{ill|Dai-Shogun Shrine|ja|大将軍|12=draft}} Shrine, Kochiho Shrine, {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Hiyoshisha|ja|日吉社|12=draft}} * Kagura Hall * Palanquin Pavilion * Shrine Gate == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|12=draft}}’s Imperial Edict Prayer Document === Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto City === * Main Hall * Offerings Hall * Worship Hall * Corridor == Festivals == Until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|12=draft}} the Mikoshi Reception was held on {{ill|July 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月18日 (旧暦)|12=draft}} and the Goryo Festival on {{ill|August 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|8月18日 (旧暦)|12=draft}}. After the Meiji period, May1 was designated as the Divine Auspice Festival (Shinkō‑sai) and the 18th as the Homecoming Festival (Kankō‑sai), with August18 established as the regular festival day on which a Tōyū performance is offered. Currently, the Homecoming Festival is held on either the third or fourth Sunday of May, and during the evening festival a children’s mikoshi is organized in collaboration with local elementary schools. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Goryo Faith|ja|御霊信仰|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimogoryo-jinja}} * [http://shimogoryo.main.jp/ Shimogoryo Shrine Official Website] {{Kyōyō Tenmangu Twenty-Five Shrine Pilgrimage}} {{Shinto Portal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Independent Shrines]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of the Twenty-Five Shrines of Kyōyō Tenmangu]] [[Category:Important Scenic Buildings of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Emperor Reigen]] [[Category:Translated Shrines and Temples]] [[Category:Shimogoryo Shrine]] [[Category:Goryo Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shimo-Goryō Shrine]] [[ja:下御霊神社]]

This gathering is considered to mark the founding of the enshrinements at both this shrine and {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. Since ancient times, it has been revered as the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial Palace|ja|京都御所|12=draft|12=draft}}. Originally, the deity was enshrined in the Goryo Hall of Shimodashimori‑ji (下出雲寺御霊堂) located in the Izumo District of Atago County ({{ill|Atago District|ja|愛宕郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郷|12=draft|12=draft}}) on the route of {{ill|Izumo Road|ja|出雲路|12=draft|12=draft}}. To the north stood {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, known as the Goryo Hall of Kamidashimori‑ji. Later, the shrine was relocated to Shinmachi Izumi, and subsequently, in Tenshō18 ({{ill|1590|ja|1590|12=draft|12=draft}}), as part of the urban development initiated by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was moved to the site where {{ill|Saionji Sanjitsu|ja|西園寺実氏|12=draft|12=draft}} had his villa, Tokiwaido. During the Kyōhō era ({{ill|1716|ja|1716|12=draft|12=draft}} – {{ill|1736|ja|1736|12=draft|12=draft}}), the spirit of {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in Kansei3 ({{ill|1791|ja|1791|12=draft|12=draft}}) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence, after the old building was destroyed in the Great Tenmei Fire of Tenmei8 ({{ill|1788|ja|1788|12=draft|12=draft}}). In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was categorized as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}}. Notably, scholar {{ill|Shiga Tsuyoshi|ja|志賀剛|12=draft|12=draft}} of Shikinai shrines has identified this shrine with {{ill|Izumo Iokami Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Shikinai Taisha) as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=draft|12=draft}}}} The enshrined deities consist of the following eight gods, collectively known as the "Eight Goryo": * Kibi Seirei (although often identified with {{ill|Kibi Mabibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft|12=draft}}, since Kibi Mabibi did not die in a state of indignation, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|Japanese spirit|ja|和魂|12=draft|12=draft}} of the six divine spirits) * Emperor Suidō (the crown prince of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Prince Hayara|ja|早良親王|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|12=draft|12=draft}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Lady Fujiwara (mother of Prince Iyo, {{ill|Fujiwara Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Fujiwara Daitō ({{ill|Fujiwara Hirotsugu|ja|藤原広嗣|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Tachibana Daifu ({{ill|Tachibana Itsusei|ja|橘逸勢|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Fumi Daifu ({{ill|Fumimuro no Miyata no Maro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|lt=Fumi Daifu|lt_ja=文屋宮田麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Karei Tenjin (although often identified with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft|12=draft}}, since the shrine was founded before he came to be regarded as Tenjin, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|rough spirit|ja|荒魂|12=draft|12=draft}} of the six divine spirits) All these deities are individuals who met their untimely deaths amid political strife, and this shrine was established to appease their vengeful spirits ({{ill|Vengeful Spirit|ja|怨霊|12=draft|12=draft}}). In the side hall, {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} is enshrined as the Tennchūbashira Imperial Deity, which is attributed to the close ties between the shrine’s then priests, the father and son {{ill|Izumori Nobu-nao|ja|出雲路信直|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izumori Naomoto|ja|出雲路直元|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the emperor. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) – Reconstructed in Kansei3 ({{ill|1791|ja|1791|12=draft|12=draft}}) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence. * Offerings Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Corridor (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Worship Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Prayer Hall * Shrine Office * Shinmeisha ({{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|lt=Naiku|lt_ja=内宮|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社|12=draft|12=draft}} – Built during the {{ill|Bunsei era|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1818|ja|1818|12=draft|12=draft}} – {{ill|1831|ja|1831|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Sarutahiko Shrine, Tarika Shrine, and Kakimoto Shrine – Enshrining, in addition to {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamazaki Ansai|ja|山崎闇斎|12=draft|12=draft}}, who advocated {{ill|Tarika Shintō|ja|垂加神道|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kakimoto no Hitomaro|ja|柿本人麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Munakata Shrine|lt_ja=宗像社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tenmangu Shrine|ja|天満宮|12=draft|12=draft}} – Enshrined deity: Kitano Ōkami * {{ill|Ōkuninushi Shrine|ja|大国主|12=draft|12=draft}} * Goshasōdensho – Saibe Shrine, {{ill|Dai-Shogun Shrine|ja|大将軍|12=draft|12=draft}} Shrine, Kochiho Shrine, {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Hiyoshisha|ja|日吉社|12=draft|12=draft}} * Kagura Hall * Palanquin Pavilion * Shrine Gate == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}’s Imperial Edict Prayer Document === Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto City === * Main Hall * Offerings Hall * Worship Hall * Corridor == Festivals == Until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} the Mikoshi Reception was held on {{ill|July 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月18日 (旧暦)|12=draft|12=draft}} and the Goryo Festival on {{ill|August 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|8月18日 (旧暦)|12=draft|12=draft}}. After the Meiji period, May1 was designated as the Divine Auspice Festival (Shinkō‑sai) and the 18th as the Homecoming Festival (Kankō‑sai), with August18 established as the regular festival day on which a Tōyū performance is offered. Currently, the Homecoming Festival is held on either the third or fourth Sunday of May, and during the evening festival a children’s mikoshi is organized in collaboration with local elementary schools. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Goryo Faith|ja|御霊信仰|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimogoryo-jinja}} * [http://shimogoryo.main.jp/ Shimogoryo Shrine Official Website] {{Kyōyō Tenmangu Twenty-Five Shrine Pilgrimage}} {{Shinto Portal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Independent Shrines]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of the Twenty-Five Shrines of Kyōyō Tenmangu]] [[Category:Important Scenic Buildings of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Emperor Reigen]] [[Category:Translated Shrines and Temples]] [[Category:Shimogoryo Shrine]] [[Category:Goryo Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shimo-Goryō Shrine]] [[ja:下御霊神社]]

It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine’s name corresponds with that of {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == During the era of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, epidemics spread throughout various regions. This was regarded as a curse ({{ill|Curse|ja|祟り|12=draft|12=draft}}) stemming from the {{ill|Goryo Faith|ja|御霊信仰|lt=Spirits|lt_ja=御霊|12=draft|12=draft}}, and accordingly, on May 20 of {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|Jōgan|12=draft|12=draft}}5 ({{ill|863|ja|863|12=draft|12=draft}}), a Goryo Gathering ({{ill|Goryo Gathering|ja|御霊会|12=draft|12=draft}}) was held at the {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京|12=draft|12=draft}} site of {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑|12=draft|12=draft}}. This gathering is considered to mark the founding of the enshrinements at both this shrine and {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. Originally, the deity was enshrined in the Goryo Hall of Shimodashimori‑ji (下出雲寺御霊堂) located in the Izumo District of Atago County ({{ill|Atago District|ja|愛宕郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郷|12=draft|12=draft}}) on the route of {{ill|Izumo Road|ja|出雲路|12=draft|12=draft}}. To the north stood {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, known as the Goryo Hall of Kamidashimori‑ji. Later, the shrine was relocated to Shinmachi Izumi, and subsequently, in Tenshō18 ({{ill|1590|ja|1590|12=draft|12=draft}}), as part of the urban development initiated by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was moved to the site where {{ill|Saionji Sanjitsu|ja|西園寺実氏|12=draft|12=draft}} had his villa, Tokiwaido. During the Kyōhō era ({{ill|1716|ja|1716|12=draft|12=draft}} – {{ill|1736|ja|1736|12=draft|12=draft}}), the spirit of {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in Kansei3 ({{ill|1791|ja|1791|12=draft|12=draft}}) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence, after the old building was destroyed in the Great Tenmei Fire of Tenmei8 ({{ill|1788|ja|1788|12=draft|12=draft}}). In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was categorized as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}}. Notably, scholar {{ill|Shiga Tsuyoshi|ja|志賀剛|12=draft|12=draft}} of Shikinai shrines has identified this shrine with {{ill|Izumo Iokami Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Shikinai Taisha) as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=draft|12=draft}}}} The enshrined deities consist of the following eight gods, collectively known as the "Eight Goryo": * Kibi Seirei (although often identified with {{ill|Kibi Mabibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft|12=draft}}, since Kibi Mabibi did not die in a state of indignation, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|Japanese spirit|ja|和魂|12=draft|12=draft}} of the six divine spirits) * Emperor Suidō (the crown prince of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Prince Hayara|ja|早良親王|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|12=draft|12=draft}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Lady Fujiwara (mother of Prince Iyo, {{ill|Fujiwara Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Fujiwara Daitō ({{ill|Fujiwara Hirotsugu|ja|藤原広嗣|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Tachibana Daifu ({{ill|Tachibana Itsusei|ja|橘逸勢|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Fumi Daifu ({{ill|Fumimuro no Miyata no Maro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|lt=Fumi Daifu|lt_ja=文屋宮田麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Karei Tenjin (although often identified with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft|12=draft}}, since the shrine was founded before he came to be regarded as Tenjin, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|rough spirit|ja|荒魂|12=draft|12=draft}} of the six divine spirits) All these deities are individuals who met their untimely deaths amid political strife, and this shrine was established to appease their vengeful spirits ({{ill|Vengeful Spirit|ja|怨霊|12=draft|12=draft}}). father and son {{ill|Izumori Nobu-nao|ja|出雲路信直|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izumori Naomoto|ja|出雲路直元|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the emperor. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall (a designated tangible Since ancient times, it has been revered as the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial Palace|ja|京都御所|12=draft|12=draft}}. Originally, the deity was enshrined in the Goryo Hall of Shimodashimori‑ji (下出雲寺御霊堂) located in the Izumo District of Atago County ({{ill|Atago District|ja|愛宕郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郷|12=draft|12=draft}}) on the route of {{ill|Izumo Road|ja|出雲路|12=draft|12=draft}}. To the north stood {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, known as the Goryo Hall of Kamidashimori‑ji. Later, the shrine was relocated to Shinmachi Izumi, and subsequently, in Tenshō18 ({{ill|1590|ja|1590|12=draft|12=draft}}), as part of the urban development initiated by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was moved to the site where {{ill|Saionji Sanjitsu|ja|西園寺実氏|12=draft|12=draft}} had his villa, Tokiwaido. During the Kyōhō era ({{ill|1716|ja|1716|12=draft|12=draft}} – {{ill|1736|ja|1736|12=draft|12=draft}}), the spirit of {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in Kansei3 ({{ill|1791|ja|1791|12=draft|12=draft}}) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence, after the old building was destroyed in the Great Tenmei Fire of Tenmei8 ({{ill|1788|ja|1788|12=draft|12=draft}}). In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was categorized as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}}. Notably, scholar {{ill|Shiga Tsuyoshi|ja|志賀剛|12=draft|12=draft}} of Shikinai shrines has identified this shrine with {{ill|Izumo Iokami Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Shikinai Taisha) as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=draft|12=draft}}}} The enshrined deities consist of the following eight gods, collectively known as the "Eight Goryo": * Kibi Seirei (although often identified with {{ill|Kibi Mabibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft|12=draft}}, since Kibi Mabibi did not die in a state of indignation, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|Japanese spirit|ja|和魂|12=draft|12=draft}} of the six divine spirits) * Emperor Suidō (the crown prince of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Prince Hayara|ja|早良親王|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|12=draft|12=draft}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Lady Fujiwara (mother of Prince Iyo, {{ill|Fujiwara Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Fujiwara Daitō ({{ill|Fujiwara Hirotsugu|ja|藤原広嗣|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Tachibana Daifu ({{ill|Tachibana Itsusei|ja|橘逸勢|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Fumi Daifu ({{ill|Fumimuro no Miyata no Maro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|lt=Fumi Daifu|lt_ja=文屋宮田麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Karei Tenjin (although often identified with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft|12=draft}}, since the shrine was founded before he came to be regarded as Tenjin, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|rough spirit|ja|荒魂|12=draft|12=draft}} of the six divine spirits) All these deities are individuals who met their untimely deaths amid political strife, and this shrine was established to appease their vengeful spirits ({{ill|Vengeful Spirit|ja|怨霊|12=draft|12=draft}}). In the side hall, {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} is enshrined as the Tennchūbashira Imperial Deity, which is attributed to the close ties between the shrine’s then priests, the father and son {{ill|Izumori Nobu-nao|ja|出雲路信直|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izumori Naomoto|ja|出雲路直元|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the emperor. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) – Reconstructed in Kansei3 ({{ill|1791|ja|1791|12=draft|12=draft}}) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence. * Offerings Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Corridor (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Worship Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Prayer Hall * Shrine Office * Shinmeisha ({{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|lt=Naiku|lt_ja=内宮|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社|12=draft|12=draft}} – Built during the {{ill|Bunsei era|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1818|ja|1818|12=draft|12=draft}} – {{ill|1831|ja|1831|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Sarutahiko Shrine, Tarika Shrine, and Kakimoto Shrine – Enshrining, in addition to {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamazaki Ansai|ja|山崎闇斎|12=draft|12=draft}}, who advocated {{ill|Tarika Shintō|ja|垂加神道|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kakimoto no Hitomaro|ja|柿本人麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Munakata Shrine|lt_ja=宗像社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tenmangu Shrine|ja|天満宮|12=draft|12=draft}} – Enshrined deity: Kitano Ōkami * {{ill|Ōkuninushi Shrine|ja|大国主|12=draft|12=draft}} * Goshasōdensho – Saibe Shrine, {{ill|Dai-Shogun Shrine|ja|大将軍|12=draft|12=draft}} Shrine, Kochiho Shrine, {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Hiyoshisha|ja|日吉社|12=draft|12=draft}} * Kagura Hall * Palanquin Pavilion * Shrine Gate == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}’s Imperial Edict Prayer Document === Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto City === * Main Hall * Offerings Hall * Worship Hall * Corridor == Festivals == Until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} the Mikoshi Reception was held on {{ill|July 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月18日 (旧暦)|12=draft|12=draft}} and the Goryo Festival on {{ill|August 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|8月18日 (旧暦)|12=draft|12=draft}}. After the Meiji period, May1 was designated as the Divine Auspice Festival (Shinkō‑sai) and the 18th as the Homecoming Festival (Kankō‑sai), with August18 established as the regular festival day on which a Tōyū performance is offered. Currently, the Homecoming Festival is held on either the third or fourth Sunday of May, and during the evening festival a children’s mikoshi is organized in collaboration with local elementary schools. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Goryo Faith|ja|御霊信仰|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimogoryo-jinja}} * [http://shimogoryo.main.jp/ Shimogoryo Shrine Official Website] {{Kyōyō Tenmangu Twenty-Five Shrine Pilgrimage}} {{Shinto Portal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Independent Shrines]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of the Twenty-Five Shrines of Kyōyō Tenmangu]] [[Category:Important Scenic Buildings of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Emperor Reigen]] [[Category:Translated Shrines and Temples]] [[Category:Shimogoryo Shrine]] [[Category:Goryo Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shimo-Goryō Shrine]] [[ja:下御霊神社]]

{{infobox religious building | name = Shimogoryo Shrine | native_name = 下御霊神社 | address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Nakagyo Ward|ja|中京区|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Teramachi Street|ja|寺町通|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Marutamachi Street|ja|丸太町通|lt=Marutamachi|lt_ja=丸太町|12=draft|12=draft}} 下る下御霊前町 | coordinates = {{coord|35|01|01|N|135|46|04|E|region:JP-26|type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shimogoryo Shrine}} | deities = 吉備聖霊
{{ill|Prince Hayara|ja|早良親王|lt=Emperor Suidō|lt_ja=崇道天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Fujiwara Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|lt=Lady Fujiwara|lt_ja=藤原大夫人|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Fujiwara Hirotsugu|ja|藤原広嗣|lt=Fujiwara Daitō|lt_ja=藤大夫|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Tachibana Itsusei|ja|橘逸勢|lt=Tachibana Daifu|lt_ja=橘大夫|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Fumimuro no Miyata no Maro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|lt=Fumi Daifu|lt_ja=文大夫|12=draft|12=draft}}
火雷天神 | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|Jōgan|12=draft|12=draft}}5th year （{{ill|863|ja|863|12=draft|12=draft}}） | status = 旧{{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}} | pilgrimage = {{ill|Kyoto-Rakuya Hachasha Shūin Meguri|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|12=draft|12=draft}} | festival = 5月第3日曜日（御霊祭） | File = [[File:Shimogoryo_torii.jpg|250px|]] }} '''Shimogoryo Shrine''' (下御霊神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Nakagyo Ward|ja|中京区|12=draft|12=draft}}, Shimogoryo‑mae Town. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine’s name corresponds with that of {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == During the era of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, epidemics spread throughout various regions. This was regarded as a curse ({{ill|Curse|ja|祟り|12=draft|12=draft}}) stemming from the {{ill|Goryo Faith|ja|御霊信仰|lt=Spirits|lt_ja=御霊|12=draft|12=draft}}, and accordingly, on May 20 of {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|Jōgan|12=draft|12=draft}}5 ({{ill|863|ja|863|12=draft|12=draft}}), a Goryo Gathering ({{ill|Goryo Gathering|ja|御霊会|12=draft|12=draft}}) was held at the {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京|12=draft|12=draft}} site of {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑|12=draft|12=draft}}. This gathering is considered to mark the founding of the enshrinements at both this shrine and {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. Since ancient times, it has been revered as the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial Palace|ja|京都御所|12=draft|12=draft}}. Originally, the deity was enshrined in the Goryo Hall of Shimodashimori‑ji (下出雲寺御霊堂) located in the Izumo District of Atago County ({{ill|Atago District|ja|愛宕郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郷|12=draft|12=draft}}) on the route of {{ill|Izumo Road|ja|出雲路|12=draft|12=draft}}. To the north stood {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, known as the Goryo Hall of Kamidashimori‑ji. Later, the shrine was relocated to Shinmachi Izumi, and subsequently, in Tenshō18 ({{ill|1590|ja|1590|12=draft|12=draft}}), as part of the urban development initiated by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was moved to the site where {{ill|Saionji Sanjitsu|ja|西園寺実氏|12=draft|12=draft}} had his villa, Tokiwaido. During the Kyōhō era ({{ill|1716|ja|1716|12=draft|12=draft}} – {{ill|1736|ja|1736|12=draft|12=draft}}), the spirit of {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in Kansei3 ({{ill|1791|ja|1791|12=draft|12=draft}}) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence, after the old building was destroyed in the Great Tenmei Fire of Tenmei8 ({{ill|1788|ja|1788|12=draft|12=draft}}). In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was categorized as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}}. Notably, scholar {{ill|Shiga Tsuyoshi|ja|志賀剛|12=draft|12=draft}} of Shikinai shrines has identified this shrine with {{ill|Izumo Iokami Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Shikinai Taisha) as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=draft|12=draft}}}} The enshrined deities consist of the following eight gods, collectively known as the "Eight Goryo": * Kibi Seirei (although often identified with {{ill|Kibi Mabibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft|12=draft}}, since Kibi Mabibi did not die in a state of indignation, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|Japanese spirit|ja|和魂|12=draft|12=draft}} of the six divine spirits) * Emperor Suidō (the crown prince of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Prince Hayara|ja|早良親王|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|12=draft|12=draft}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Lady Fujiwara (mother of Prince Iyo, {{ill|Fujiwara Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Fujiwara Daitō ({{ill|Fujiwara Hirotsugu|ja|藤原広嗣|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Tachibana Daifu ({{ill|Tachibana Itsusei|ja|橘逸勢|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Fumi Daifu ({{ill|Fumimuro no Miyata no Maro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|lt=Fumi Daifu|lt_ja=文屋宮田麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Karei Tenjin (although often identified with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft|12=draft}}, since the shrine was founded before he came to be regarded as Tenjin, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|rough spirit|ja|荒魂|12=draft|12=draft}} of the six divine spirits) All these deities are individuals who met their untimely deaths amid political strife, and this shrine was established to appease their vengeful spirits ({{ill|Vengeful Spirit|ja|怨霊|12=draft|12=draft}}). In the side hall, {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} is enshrined as the Tennchūbashira Imperial Deity, which is attributed to the close ties between the shrine’s then priests, the father and son {{ill|Izumori Nobu-nao|ja|出雲路信直|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izumori Naomoto|ja|出雲路直元|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the emperor. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) – Reconstructed in Kansei3 ({{ill|1791|ja|1791|12=draft|12=draft}}) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence. * Offerings Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Corridor (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Worship Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Prayer Hall * Shrine Office * Shinmeisha ({{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|lt=Naiku|lt_ja=内宮|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社|12=draft|12=draft}} – Built during the {{ill|Bunsei era|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1818|ja|1818|12=draft|12=draft}} – {{ill|1831|ja|1831|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Sarutahiko Shrine, Tarika Shrine, and Kakimoto Shrine – Enshrining, in addition to {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamazaki Ansai|ja|山崎闇斎|12=draft|12=draft}}, who advocated {{ill|Tarika Shintō|ja|垂加神道|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kakimoto no Hitomaro|ja|柿本人麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Munakata Shrine|lt_ja=宗像社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tenmangu Shrine|ja|天満宮|12=draft|12=draft}} – Enshrined deity: Kitano Ōkami * {{ill|Ōkuninushi Shrine|ja|大国主|12=draft|12=draft}} * Goshasōdensho – Saibe Shrine, {{ill|Dai-Shogun Shrine|ja|大将軍|12=draft|12=draft}} Shrine, Kochiho Shrine, {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Hiyoshisha|ja|日吉社|12=draft|12=draft}} * Kagura Hall * Palanquin Pavilion * Shrine Gate == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}’s Imperial Edict Prayer Document === Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto City === * Main Hall * Offerings Hall * Worship Hall * Corridor == Festivals == Until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} the Mikoshi Reception was held on {{ill|July 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月18日 (旧暦)|12=draft|12=draft}} and the Goryo Festival on {{ill|August 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|8月18日 (旧暦)|12=draft|12=draft}}. After the Meiji period, May1 was designated as the Divine Auspice Festival (Shinkō‑sai) and the 18th as the Homecoming Festival (Kankō‑sai), with August18 established as the regular festival day on which a Tōyū performance is offered. Currently, the Homecoming Festival is held on either the third or fourth Sunday of May, and during the evening festival a children’s mikoshi is organized in collaboration with local elementary schools. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Goryo Faith|ja|御霊信仰|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimogoryo-jinja}} * [http://shimogoryo.main.jp/ Shimogoryo Shrine Official Website] {{Kyōyō Tenmangu Twenty-Five Shrine Pilgrimage}} {{Shinto Portal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Independent Shrines]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of the Twenty-Five Shrines of Kyōyō Tenmangu]] [[Category:Important Scenic Buildings of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Emperor Reigen]] [[Category:Translated Shrines and Temples]] [[Category:Shimogoryo Shrine]] [[Category:Goryo Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shimo-Goryō Shrine]] [[ja:下御霊神社]]
