This gathering is considered to mark the founding of the enshrinements at both this shrine and {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. Since ancient times, it has been revered as the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial Palace|ja|京都御所|12=draft|12=draft}}. Originally, the deity was enshrined in the Goryo Hall of Shimodashimori‑ji (下出雲寺御霊堂) located in the Izumo District of Atago County ({{ill|Atago District|ja|愛宕郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郷|12=draft|12=draft}}) on the route of {{ill|Izumo Road|ja|出雲路|12=draft|12=draft}}. To the north stood {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, known as the Goryo Hall of Kamidashimori‑ji. Later, the shrine was relocated to Shinmachi Izumi, and subsequently, in Tenshō18 ({{ill|1590|ja|1590|12=draft|12=draft}}), as part of the urban development initiated by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was moved to the site where {{ill|Saionji Sanjitsu|ja|西園寺実氏|12=draft|12=draft}} had his villa, Tokiwaido. During the Kyōhō era ({{ill|1716|ja|1716|12=draft|12=draft}} – {{ill|1736|ja|1736|12=draft|12=draft}}), the spirit of {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in Kansei3 ({{ill|1791|ja|1791|12=draft|12=draft}}) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence, after the old building was destroyed in the Great Tenmei Fire of Tenmei8 ({{ill|1788|ja|1788|12=draft|12=draft}}). In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was categorized as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}}. Notably, scholar {{ill|Shiga Tsuyoshi|ja|志賀剛|12=draft|12=draft}} of Shikinai shrines has identified this shrine with {{ill|Izumo Iokami Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Shikinai Taisha) as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=draft|12=draft}}}} The enshrined deities consist of the following eight gods, collectively known as the "Eight Goryo": * Kibi Seirei (although often identified with {{ill|Kibi Mabibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft|12=draft}}, since Kibi Mabibi did not die in a state of indignation, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|Japanese spirit|ja|和魂|12=draft|12=draft}} of the six divine spirits) * Emperor Suidō (the crown prince of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Prince Hayara|ja|早良親王|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|12=draft|12=draft}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Lady Fujiwara (mother of Prince Iyo, {{ill|Fujiwara Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Fujiwara Daitō ({{ill|Fujiwara Hirotsugu|ja|藤原広嗣|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Tachibana Daifu ({{ill|Tachibana Itsusei|ja|橘逸勢|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Fumi Daifu ({{ill|Fumimuro no Miyata no Maro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|lt=Fumi Daifu|lt_ja=文屋宮田麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Karei Tenjin (although often identified with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft|12=draft}}, since the shrine was founded before he came to be regarded as Tenjin, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|rough spirit|ja|荒魂|12=draft|12=draft}} of the six divine spirits) All these deities are individuals who met their untimely deaths amid political strife, and this shrine was established to appease their vengeful spirits ({{ill|Vengeful Spirit|ja|怨霊|12=draft|12=draft}}). In the side hall, {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} is enshrined as the Tennchūbashira Imperial Deity, which is attributed to the close ties between the shrine’s then priests, the father and son {{ill|Izumori Nobu-nao|ja|出雲路信直|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izumori Naomoto|ja|出雲路直元|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the emperor. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) – Reconstructed in Kansei3 ({{ill|1791|ja|1791|12=draft|12=draft}}) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence. * Offerings Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Corridor (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Worship Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Prayer Hall * Shrine Office * Shinmeisha ({{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|lt=Naiku|lt_ja=内宮|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社|12=draft|12=draft}} – Built during the {{ill|Bunsei era|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1818|ja|1818|12=draft|12=draft}} – {{ill|1831|ja|1831|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Sarutahiko Shrine, Tarika Shrine, and Kakimoto Shrine – Enshrining, in addition to {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamazaki Ansai|ja|山崎闇斎|12=draft|12=draft}}, who advocated {{ill|Tarika Shintō|ja|垂加神道|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kakimoto no Hitomaro|ja|柿本人麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Munakata Shrine|lt_ja=宗像社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tenmangu Shrine|ja|天満宮|12=draft|12=draft}} – Enshrined deity: Kitano Ōkami * {{ill|Ōkuninushi Shrine|ja|大国主|12=draft|12=draft}} * Goshasōdensho – Saibe Shrine, {{ill|Dai-Shogun Shrine|ja|大将軍|12=draft|12=draft}} Shrine, Kochiho Shrine, {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Hiyoshisha|ja|日吉社|12=draft|12=draft}} * Kagura Hall * Palanquin Pavilion * Shrine Gate == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}’s Imperial Edict Prayer Document === Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto City === * Main Hall * Offerings Hall * Worship Hall * Corridor == Festivals == Until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} the Mikoshi Reception was held on {{ill|July 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月18日 (旧暦)|12=draft|12=draft}} and the Goryo Festival on {{ill|August 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|8月18日 (旧暦)|12=draft|12=draft}}. After the Meiji period, May1 was designated as the Divine Auspice Festival (Shinkō‑sai) and the 18th as the Homecoming Festival (Kankō‑sai), with August18 established as the regular festival day on which a Tōyū performance is offered. Currently, the Homecoming Festival is held on either the third or fourth Sunday of May, and during the evening festival a children’s mikoshi is organized in collaboration with local elementary schools. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Goryo Faith|ja|御霊信仰|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimogoryo-jinja}} * [http://shimogoryo.main.jp/ Shimogoryo Shrine Official Website] {{Kyōyō Tenmangu Twenty-Five Shrine Pilgrimage}} {{Shinto Portal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Independent Shrines]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of the Twenty-Five Shrines of Kyōyō Tenmangu]] [[Category:Important Scenic Buildings of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Emperor Reigen]] [[Category:Translated Shrines and Temples]] [[Category:Shimogoryo Shrine]] [[Category:Goryo Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shimo-Goryō Shrine]] [[ja:下御霊神社]]

火雷天神 | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|Jōgan|12=draft|12=draft}}5th year （{{ill|863|ja|863|12=draft|12=draft}}） | status = 旧{{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}} | pilgrimage = {{ill|Kyoto-Rakuya Hachasha Shūin Meguri|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|12=draft|12=draft}} | festival = 5月第3日曜日（御霊祭） | File = [[File:Shimogoryo_torii.jpg|250px|]] }} '''Shimogoryo Shrine''' (下御霊神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Nakagyo Ward|ja|中京区|12=draft|12=draft}}, Shimogoryo‑mae Town. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine’s name corresponds with that of {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == During the era of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, epidemics spread throughout various regions. This was regarded as a curse ({{ill|Curse|ja|祟り|12=draft|12=draft}}) stemming from the {{ill|Goryo Faith|ja|御霊信仰|lt=Spirits|lt_ja=御霊|12=draft|12=draft}}, and accordingly, on May 20 of {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|Jōgan|12=draft|12=draft}}5 ({{ill|863|ja|863|12=draft|12=draft}}), a Goryo Gathering ({{ill|Goryo Gathering|ja|御霊会|12=draft|12=draft}}) was held at the {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京|12=draft|12=draft}} site of {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑|12=draft|12=draft}}. This gathering is considered to mark the founding of the enshrinements at both this shrine and {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. Since ancient times, it has been revered as the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial Palace|ja|京都御所|12=draft|12=draft}}. Originally, the deity was enshrined in the Goryo Hall of Shimodashimori‑ji (下出雲寺御霊堂) located in the Izumo District of Atago County ({{ill|Atago District|ja|愛宕郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郷|12=draft|12=draft}}) on the route of {{ill|Izumo Road|ja|出雲路|12=draft|12=draft}}. To the north stood {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, known as the Goryo Hall of Kamidashimori‑ji. Later, the shrine was relocated to Shinmachi Izumi, and subsequently, in Tenshō18 ({{ill|1590|ja|1590|12=draft|12=draft}}), as part of the urban development initiated by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was moved to the site where {{ill|Saionji Sanjitsu|ja|西園寺実氏|12=draft|12=draft}} had his villa, Tokiwaido. During the Kyōhō era ({{ill|1716|ja|1716|12=draft|12=draft}} – {{ill|1736|ja|1736|12=draft|12=draft}}), the spirit of {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in Kansei3 ({{ill|1791|ja|1791|12=draft|12=draft}}) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence, after the old building was destroyed in the Great Tenmei Fire of Tenmei8 ({{ill|1788|ja|1788|12=draft|12=draft}}). In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was categorized as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}}. Notably, scholar {{ill|Shiga Tsuyoshi|ja|志賀剛|12=draft|12=draft}} of Shikinai shrines has identified this shrine with {{ill|Izumo Iokami Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Shikinai Taisha) as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=draft|12=draft}}}} The enshrined deities consist of the following eight gods, collectively known as the "Eight Goryo": * Kibi Seirei (although often identified with {{ill|Kibi Mabibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft|12=draft}}, since Kibi Mabibi did not die in a state of indignation, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|Japanese spirit|ja|和魂|12=draft|12=draft}} of the six divine spirits) * Emperor Suidō (the crown prince of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Prince Hayara|ja|早良親王|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|12=draft|12=draft}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Lady Fujiwara (mother of Prince Iyo, {{ill|Fujiwara Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Fujiwara Daitō ({{ill|Fujiwara Hirotsugu|ja|藤原広嗣|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Tachibana Daifu ({{ill|Tachibana Itsusei|ja|橘逸勢|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Fumi Daifu Michizane|12=draft|12=draft}}, since the shrine was founded before he came to be regarded as Tenjin, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|rough spirit|ja|荒魂|12=draft|12=draft}} of the six divine spirits) All these deities are individuals who met their untimely deaths amid political strife, and this shrine was established to appease their vengeful spirits ({{ill|Vengeful In the side hall, {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} is enshrined as the Tennchūbashira Imperial Deity, which is attributed to the close ties between the shrine’s then priests, the father and son {{ill|Izumori Nobu-nao|ja|出雲路信直|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izumori Naomoto|ja|出雲路直元|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the emperor. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall (a designated tangible Kyoto City) * Prayer Hall * Shrine Office * Shinmeisha ({{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|lt=Naiku|lt_ja=内宮|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社|12=draft|12=draft}} – Built during the {{ill|Bunsei era|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1818|ja|1818|12=draft|12=draft}} – 火雷天神 | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|Jōgan|12=draft|12=draft}}5th year （{{ill|863|ja|863|12=draft|12=draft}}） | status = 旧{{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}} | pilgrimage = {{ill|Kyoto-Rakuya Hachasha Shūin Meguri|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|12=draft|12=draft}} | festival = 5月第3日曜日（御霊祭） | File = [[File:Shimogoryo_torii.jpg|250px|]] }} '''Shimogoryo Shrine''' (下御霊神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Nakagyo Ward|ja|中京区|12=draft|12=draft}}, Shimogoryo‑mae Town. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine’s name corresponds with that of {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == During the era of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, epidemics spread throughout various regions. This was regarded as a curse ({{ill|Curse|ja|祟り|12=draft|12=draft}}) stemming from the {{ill|Goryo Faith|ja|御霊信仰|lt=Spirits|lt_ja=御霊|12=draft|12=draft}}, and accordingly, on May 20 of {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|Jōgan|12=draft|12=draft}}5 ({{ill|863|ja|863|12=draft|12=draft}}), a Goryo Gathering ({{ill|Goryo Gathering|ja|御霊会|12=draft|12=draft}}) was held at the {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京|12=draft|12=draft}} site of {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑|12=draft|12=draft}}. This gathering is considered to mark the founding of the enshrinements at both this shrine and {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. Since ancient times, it has been revered as the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial Palace|ja|京都御所|12=draft|12=draft}}. Originally, the deity was enshrined in the Goryo Hall of Shimodashimori‑ji (下出雲寺御霊堂) located in the Izumo District of Atago County ({{ill|Atago District|ja|愛宕郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郷|12=draft|12=draft}}) on the route of {{ill|Izumo Road|ja|出雲路|12=draft|12=draft}}. To the north stood {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, known as the Goryo Hall of Kamidashimori‑ji. Later, the shrine was relocated to Shinmachi Izumi, and subsequently, in Tenshō18 ({{ill|1590|ja|1590|12=draft|12=draft}}), as part of the urban development initiated by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was moved to the site where {{ill|Saionji Sanjitsu|ja|西園寺実氏|12=draft|12=draft}} had his villa, Tokiwaido. During the Kyōhō era ({{ill|1716|ja|1716|12=draft|12=draft}} – {{ill|1736|ja|1736|12=draft|12=draft}}), the spirit of {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in Kansei3 ({{ill|1791|ja|1791|12=draft|12=draft}}) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence, after the old building was destroyed in the Great Tenmei Fire of Tenmei8 ({{ill|1788|ja|1788|12=draft|12=draft}}). In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was categorized as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}}. Notably, scholar {{ill|Shiga Tsuyoshi|ja|志賀剛|12=draft|12=draft}} of Shikinai shrines has identified this shrine with {{ill|Izumo Iokami Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Shikinai Taisha) as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=draft|12=draft}}}} The enshrined deities consist of the following eight gods, collectively known as the "Eight Goryo": * Kibi Seirei (although often identified with {{ill|Kibi Mabibi|ja|吉備真備|12=draft|12=draft}}, since Kibi Mabibi did not die in a state of indignation, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|Japanese spirit|ja|和魂|12=draft|12=draft}} of the six divine spirits) * Emperor Suidō (the crown prince of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Prince Hayara|ja|早良親王|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|12=draft|12=draft}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Lady Fujiwara (mother of Prince Iyo, {{ill|Fujiwara Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Fujiwara Daitō ({{ill|Fujiwara Hirotsugu|ja|藤原広嗣|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Tachibana Daifu ({{ill|Tachibana Itsusei|ja|橘逸勢|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Fumi Daifu ({{ill|Fumimuro no Miyata no Maro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|lt=Fumi Daifu|lt_ja=文屋宮田麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Karei Tenjin (although often identified with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft|12=draft}}, since the shrine was founded before he came to be regarded as Tenjin, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|rough spirit|ja|荒魂|12=draft|12=draft}} of the six divine spirits) All these deities are individuals who met their untimely deaths amid political strife, and this shrine was established to appease their vengeful spirits ({{ill|Vengeful Spirit|ja|怨霊|12=draft|12=draft}}). In the side hall, {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} is enshrined as the Tennchūbashira Imperial Deity, which is attributed to the close ties between the shrine’s then priests, the father and son {{ill|Izumori Nobu-nao|ja|出雲路信直|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izumori Naomoto|ja|出雲路直元|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the emperor. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) – Reconstructed in Kansei3 ({{ill|1791|ja|1791|12=draft|12=draft}}) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence. * Offerings Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Corridor (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Worship Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Prayer Hall * Shrine Office * Shinmeisha ({{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|lt=Naiku|lt_ja=内宮|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=draft|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社|12=draft|12=draft}} – Built during the {{ill|Bunsei era|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1818|ja|1818|12=draft|12=draft}} – {{ill|1831|ja|1831|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Sarutahiko Shrine, Tarika Shrine, and Kakimoto Shrine – Enshrining, in addition to {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamazaki Ansai|ja|山崎闇斎|12=draft|12=draft}}, who advocated {{ill|Tarika Shintō|ja|垂加神道|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kakimoto no Hitomaro|ja|柿本人麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Munakata Shrine|lt_ja=宗像社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tenmangu Shrine|ja|天満宮|12=draft|12=draft}} – Enshrined deity: Kitano Ōkami * {{ill|Ōkuninushi Shrine|ja|大国主|12=draft|12=draft}} * Goshasōdensho – Saibe Shrine, {{ill|Dai-Shogun Shrine|ja|大将軍|12=draft|12=draft}} Shrine, Kochiho Shrine, {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Hiyoshisha|ja|日吉社|12=draft|12=draft}} * Kagura Hall * Palanquin Pavilion * Shrine Gate == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}’s Imperial Edict Prayer Document === Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto City === * Main Hall * Offerings Hall * Worship Hall * Corridor == Festivals == Until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} the Mikoshi Reception was held on {{ill|July 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月18日 (旧暦)|12=draft|12=draft}} and the Goryo Festival on {{ill|August 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|8月18日 (旧暦)|12=draft|12=draft}}. After the Meiji period, May1 was designated as the Divine Auspice Festival (Shinkō‑sai) and the 18th as the Homecoming Festival (Kankō‑sai), with August18 established as the regular festival day on which a Tōyū performance is offered. Currently, the Homecoming Festival is held on either the third or fourth Sunday of May, and during the evening festival a children’s mikoshi is organized in collaboration with local elementary schools. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Goryo Faith|ja|御霊信仰|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimogoryo-jinja}} * [http://shimogoryo.main.jp/ Shimogoryo Shrine Official Website] {{Kyōyō Tenmangu Twenty-Five Shrine Pilgrimage}} {{Shinto Portal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Independent Shrines]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of the Twenty-Five Shrines of Kyōyō Tenmangu]] [[Category:Important Scenic Buildings of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Emperor Reigen]] [[Category:Translated Shrines and Temples]] [[Category:Shimogoryo Shrine]] [[Category:Goryo Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shimo-Goryō Shrine]] [[ja:下御霊神社]]

After the Meiji period, May1 was designated as the Divine Auspice Festival (Shinkō‑sai) and the 18th as the Homecoming Festival (Kankō‑sai), with August18 established as the regular festival day on which a Tōyū performance is offered. Currently, the Homecoming Festival is held on either the third or fourth Sunday of May, and during the evening festival a children’s mikoshi is organized in collaboration with local elementary schools. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Goryo Faith|ja|御霊信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimogoryo-jinja}} * [http://shimogoryo.main.jp/ Shimogoryo Shrine Official Website] {{Kyōyō Tenmangu Twenty-Five Shrine Pilgrimage}} {{Shinto Portal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Independent Shrines]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of the Twenty-Five Shrines of Kyōyō Tenmangu]] [[Category:Important Scenic Buildings of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Emperor Reigen]] [[Category:Translated Shrines and Temples]] [[Category:Shimogoryo Shrine]] [[Category:Goryo Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shimo-Goryō Shrine]] [[ja:下御霊神社]]

{{infobox religious building | name = Shimogoryo Shrine | native_name = 下御霊神社 | address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Nakagyo Ward|ja|中京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Teramachi Street|ja|寺町通|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Marutamachi Street|ja|丸太町通|lt=Marutamachi|lt_ja=丸太町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 下る下御霊前町 | coordinates = {{coord|35|01|01|N|135|46|04|E|region:JP-26|type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shimogoryo Shrine}} | deities = 吉備聖霊
{{ill|Prince Hayara|ja|早良親王|lt=Emperor Suidō|lt_ja=崇道天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Fujiwara Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|lt=Lady Fujiwara|lt_ja=藤原大夫人|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Fujiwara Hirotsugu|ja|藤原広嗣|lt=Fujiwara Daitō|lt_ja=藤大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Tachibana Itsusei|ja|橘逸勢|lt=Tachibana Daifu|lt_ja=橘大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Fumimuro no Miyata no Maro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|lt=Fumi Daifu|lt_ja=文大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
火雷天神 | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|Jōgan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}5th year （{{ill|863|ja|863|qq=draft|qq=draft}}） | status = 旧{{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | pilgrimage = {{ill|Kyoto-Rakuya Hachasha Shūin Meguri|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | festival = 5月第3日曜日（御霊祭） | File = [[File:Shimogoryo_torii.jpg|250px|]] }} '''Shimogoryo Shrine''' (下御霊神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Nakagyo Ward|ja|中京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Shimogoryo‑mae Town. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine’s name corresponds with that of {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == During the era of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, epidemics spread throughout various regions. This was regarded as a curse ({{ill|Curse|ja|祟り|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) stemming from the {{ill|Goryo Faith|ja|御霊信仰|lt=Spirits|lt_ja=御霊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and accordingly, on May 20 of {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|Jōgan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}5 ({{ill|863|ja|863|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), a Goryo Gathering ({{ill|Goryo Gathering|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was held at the {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京|qq=draft|qq=draft}} site of {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This gathering is considered to mark the founding of the enshrinements at both this shrine and {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Since ancient times, it has been revered as the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial Palace|ja|京都御所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Originally, the deity was enshrined in the Goryo Hall of Shimodashimori‑ji (下出雲寺御霊堂) located in the Izumo District of Atago County ({{ill|Atago District|ja|愛宕郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) on the route of {{ill|Izumo Road|ja|出雲路|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. To the north stood {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, known as the Goryo Hall of Kamidashimori‑ji. Later, the shrine was relocated to Shinmachi Izumi, and subsequently, in Tenshō18 ({{ill|1590|ja|1590|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), as part of the urban development initiated by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was moved to the site where {{ill|Saionji Sanjitsu|ja|西園寺実氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} had his villa, Tokiwaido. During the Kyōhō era ({{ill|1716|ja|1716|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – {{ill|1736|ja|1736|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the spirit of {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in Kansei3 ({{ill|1791|ja|1791|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence, after the old building was destroyed in the Great Tenmei Fire of Tenmei8 ({{ill|1788|ja|1788|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was categorized as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Notably, scholar {{ill|Shiga Tsuyoshi|ja|志賀剛|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of Shikinai shrines has identified this shrine with {{ill|Izumo Iokami Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shikinai Taisha) as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} The enshrined deities consist of the following eight gods, collectively known as the "Eight Goryo": * Kibi Seirei (although often identified with {{ill|Kibi Mabibi|ja|吉備真備|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, since Kibi Mabibi did not die in a state of indignation, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|Japanese spirit|ja|和魂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the six divine spirits) * Emperor Suidō (the crown prince of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Prince Hayara|ja|早良親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Lady Fujiwara (mother of Prince Iyo, {{ill|Fujiwara Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Fujiwara Daitō ({{ill|Fujiwara Hirotsugu|ja|藤原広嗣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Tachibana Daifu ({{ill|Tachibana Itsusei|ja|橘逸勢|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Fumi Daifu ({{ill|Fumimuro no Miyata no Maro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|lt=Fumi Daifu|lt_ja=文屋宮田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Karei Tenjin (although often identified with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, since the shrine was founded before he came to be regarded as Tenjin, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|rough spirit|ja|荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the six divine spirits) All these deities are individuals who met their untimely deaths amid political strife, and this shrine was established to appease their vengeful spirits ({{ill|Vengeful Spirit|ja|怨霊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). In the side hall, {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is enshrined as the Tennchūbashira Imperial Deity, which is attributed to the close ties between the shrine’s then priests, the father and son {{ill|Izumori Nobu-nao|ja|出雲路信直|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Izumori Naomoto|ja|出雲路直元|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the emperor. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) – Reconstructed in Kansei3 ({{ill|1791|ja|1791|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence. * Offerings Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Corridor (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Worship Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Prayer Hall * Shrine Office * Shinmeisha ({{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|lt=Naiku|lt_ja=内宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Built during the {{ill|Bunsei era|ja|文政|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1818|ja|1818|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – {{ill|1831|ja|1831|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Sarutahiko Shrine, Tarika Shrine, and Kakimoto Shrine – Enshrining, in addition to {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamazaki Ansai|ja|山崎闇斎|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who advocated {{ill|Tarika Shintō|ja|垂加神道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kakimoto no Hitomaro|ja|柿本人麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Munakata Shrine|lt_ja=宗像社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tenmangu Shrine|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined deity: Kitano Ōkami * {{ill|Ōkuninushi Shrine|ja|大国主|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Goshasōdensho – Saibe Shrine, {{ill|Dai-Shogun Shrine|ja|大将軍|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Shrine, Kochiho Shrine, {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Hiyoshisha|ja|日吉社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Kagura Hall * Palanquin Pavilion * Shrine Gate == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}’s Imperial Edict Prayer Document === Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto City === * Main Hall * Offerings Hall * Worship Hall * Corridor == Festivals == Until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} the Mikoshi Reception was held on {{ill|July 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月18日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Goryo Festival on {{ill|August 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|8月18日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. After the Meiji period, May1 was designated as the Divine Auspice Festival (Shinkō‑sai) and the 18th as the Homecoming Festival (Kankō‑sai), with August18 established as the regular festival day on which a Tōyū performance is offered. Currently, the Homecoming Festival is held on either the third or fourth Sunday of May, and during the evening festival a children’s mikoshi is organized in collaboration with local elementary schools. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Goryo Faith|ja|御霊信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimogoryo-jinja}} * [http://shimogoryo.main.jp/ Shimogoryo Shrine Official Website] {{Kyōyō Tenmangu Twenty-Five Shrine Pilgrimage}} {{Shinto Portal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Independent Shrines]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of the Twenty-Five Shrines of Kyōyō Tenmangu]] [[Category:Important Scenic Buildings of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Emperor Reigen]] [[Category:Translated Shrines and Temples]] [[Category:Shimogoryo Shrine]] [[Category:Goryo Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Shimo-Goryō Shrine]] [[ja:下御霊神社]]

revision.comment: 61 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:下御霊神社 up to rev 101246179
revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称=下御霊神社 | 所在地=[[京都府]][[京都市]][[中京区]][[寺町通]][[丸太町通|丸太町]]下る下御霊前町 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|01|01|N|135|46|04|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=下御霊神社}} | 祭神=吉備聖霊
[[早良親王|崇道天皇]]
[[伊予親王]]
[[藤原吉子|藤原大夫人]]
[[藤原広嗣|藤大夫]]
[[橘逸勢|橘大夫]]
[[文室宮田麻呂|文大夫]]
火雷天神 | 創建=[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]5年（[[863年]]） | 社格=旧[[府社]] | 札所等=[[京洛八社集印めぐり]] | 例祭=5月第3日曜日（御霊祭） | 画像=[[File:Shimogoryo_torii.jpg|250px|]] }} '''下御霊神社'''（しもごりょうじんじゃ）は、[[京都市]][[中京区]]下御霊前町にある[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[府社]]。社名は[[上御霊神社]]に呼応するものである。 == 歴史 == [[桓武天皇]]の時代、各地で疫病が流行した。これは[[御霊信仰|御霊]]の[[祟り]]であるとして、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]5年（[[863年]]）5月20日、[[平安京]]の[[神泉苑]]で[[御霊会]]が催された。この御霊会が当社および[[上御霊神社]]の創祀であるとしている。古来、[[京都御所]]の[[産土神]]として崇敬された。 元は[[愛宕郡]]出雲郷の[[出雲路]]にある下出雲寺御霊堂に祀られていた。北には上出雲寺御霊堂と呼ばれていた[[上御霊神社]]があった。後に新町出水に移され、その後、[[天正]]18年（[[1590年]]）に[[豊臣秀吉]]の都市整備にともない現在地である[[西園寺実氏]]の別荘・常盤井殿があった地に遷座した。 [[享保]]年間（[[1716年]] - [[1736年]]）には[[霊元天皇]]の霊を配祀している。 現社殿は[[天明]]8年（[[1788年]]）の[[天明の大火]]で旧社殿が焼失したのち、仮皇居の[[内侍所]]仮殿を[[寛政]]3年（[[1791年]]）に移建したものである。 [[明治]]時代になると[[府社]]に列せられている。 なお、式内社研究家の[[志賀剛]]は[[延喜式神名帳]]に記載される出雲井於神社（式内大社）を当社に比定している。 == 祭神 == 祭神は以下の八柱で、「八所御霊」と称される。 * 吉備聖霊（[[吉備真備]]とされることも多いが、吉備真備は憤死した人ではないので、神社側は六座の神霊の[[和魂]]と解釈している） * 崇道天皇（[[桓武天皇]]の皇太子、[[早良親王]]） * [[伊予親王]]（桓武天皇の皇子） * 藤原大夫人（伊予親王の母、[[藤原吉子]]） * 藤大夫（[[藤原広嗣]]） * 橘大夫（[[橘逸勢]]） * 文大夫（[[文室宮田麻呂|文屋宮田麻呂]]） * 火雷天神（[[菅原道真]]とされることも多いが、神社の創建は道真が天神とされるよりも以前なので、神社側は六座の神霊の[[荒魂]]と解釈している） これらの諸神はいずれも政争に巻き込まれて憤死した人々で、その[[怨霊]]を慰めるために創建されたのが当社である。 相殿に天中柱皇神として[[霊元天皇]]が祀られているが、これは当時の神主である[[出雲路信直]]・[[出雲路直元|直元]]父子が天皇と親交があったことによると考えられている山口和夫「霊元院政について」（初出：今谷明・高埜利彦 編『中近世の宗教と国家』（岩田書院、1998年）/所収：山口『近世日本政治史と朝廷』（吉川弘文館、2017年） ISBN 978-4-642-03480-7） 2017年、P229-230。 == 境内 == * 本殿（京都市指定有形文化財） - [[寛政]]3年（[[1791年]]）に仮皇居の[[内侍所]]仮殿を移築したもの。 * 幣殿（京都市指定有形文化財） * 廻廊（京都市指定有形文化財） * 拝所（京都市指定有形文化財） * 拝殿 * 社務所 * 神明社（[[皇大神宮|内宮]]・[[外宮]]） * [[八幡社]] * [[春日社]] - [[文政]]年間（[[1818年]] - [[1831年]]）建立。 * [[猿田彦]]社・垂加社・柿本社 - [[猿田彦]]の他、[[垂加神道]]を唱えた[[山崎闇斎]]と[[柿本人麻呂]]を祀る。 * [[稲荷社]] * [[宗像三女神|宗像社]] * [[天満宮]]社 - 祭神：北野大神 * [[大国主]]社 * 五社相殿社 - 斎部社、[[大将軍]]社、高知穂社、[[愛宕社]]、[[日吉社]] * 神楽所 * 鳳輿舎 * 神門 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財 === * [[霊元天皇]]宸翰御祈願文 === 京都市指定有形文化財 === * 本殿 * 幣殿 * 拝所 * 廻廊 == 祭事 == [[明治]]までは神輿迎が[[7月18日 (旧暦)|7月18日]]に御霊祭は[[8月18日 (旧暦)|8月18日]]に行われていたが、明治以降は5月1日が神幸祭、18日が還幸祭と改められ、8月18日は例祭日と定められて東遊が奉奏される。現在では還幸祭は5月の第3か第4日曜日に行われており、宵宮では地域の小学校と連携した子供神輿も催されている。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[御霊信仰]] * [[上御霊神社]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat| Shimogoryō-jinja}} *[http://shimogoryo.main.jp/ 下御霊神社オフィシャルサイト] {{洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しもこりようしんしや}} [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧府社]] [[Category:単立神社]] [[Category:中京区の歴史]] [[Category:中京区の建築物]] [[Category:洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝]] [[Category:京都府の景観重要建造物]] [[Category:京都市の重要文化財]] [[Category:霊元天皇]]

火雷天神 | 創建=[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]5年（[[863年]]） | 社格=旧[[府社]] | 札所等=[[京洛八社集印めぐり]] | 例祭=5月第3日曜日（御霊祭） | 画像=[[File:Shimogoryo_torii.jpg|250px|]] }} '''下御霊神社'''（しもごりょうじんじゃ）は、[[京都市]][[中京区]]下御霊前町にある[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[府社]]。社名は[[上御霊神社]]に呼応するものである。 == 歴史 == [[桓武天皇]]の時代、各地で疫病が流行した。これは[[御霊信仰|御霊]]の[[祟り]]であるとして、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]5年（[[863年]]）5月20日、[[平安京]]の[[神泉苑]]で[[御霊会]]が催された。この御霊会が当社および[[上御霊神社]]の創祀であるとしている。古来、[[京都御所]]の[[産土神]]として崇敬された。 元は[[愛宕郡]]出雲郷の[[出雲路]]にある下出雲寺御霊堂に祀られていた。北には上出雲寺御霊堂と呼ばれていた[[上御霊神社]]があった。後に新町出水に移され、その後、[[天正]]18年（[[1590年]]）に[[豊臣秀吉]]の都市整備にともない現在地である[[西園寺実氏]]の別荘・常盤井殿があった地に遷座した。 [[享保]]年間（[[1716年]] - [[1736年]]）には[[霊元天皇]]の霊を配祀している。 現社殿は[[天明]]8年（[[1788年]]）の[[天明の大火]]で旧社殿が焼失したのち、仮皇居の[[内侍所]]仮殿を[[寛政]]3年（[[1791年]]）に移建したものである。 [[明治]]時代になると[[府社]]に列せられている。 なお、式内社研究家の[[志賀剛]]は[[延喜式神名帳]]に記載される出雲井於神社（式内大社）を当社に比定している。 == 祭神 == 祭神は以下の八柱で、「八所御霊」と称される。 * 吉備聖霊（[[吉備真備]]とされることも多いが、吉備真備は憤死した人ではないので、神社側は六座の神霊の[[和魂]]と解釈している） * 崇道天皇（[[桓武天皇]]の皇太子、[[早良親王]]） * [[伊予親王]]（桓武天皇の皇子） * 藤原大夫人（伊予親王の母、[[藤原吉子]]） * 藤大夫（[[藤原広嗣]]） * 橘大夫（[[橘逸勢]]） * 文大夫（[[文室宮田麻呂|文屋宮田麻呂]]） * 火雷天神（[[菅原道真]]とされることも多いが、神社の創建は道真が天神とされるよりも以前なので、神社側は六座の神霊の[[荒魂]]と解釈している） これらの諸神はいずれも政争に巻き込まれて憤死した人々で、その[[怨霊]]を慰めるために創建されたのが当社である。 相殿に天中柱皇神として[[霊元天皇]]が祀られているが、これは当時の神主である[[出雲路信直]]・[[出雲路直元|直元]]父子が天皇と親交があったことによると考えられている山口和夫「霊元院政について」（初出：今谷明・高埜利彦 編『中近世の宗教と国家』（岩田書院、1998年）/所収：山口『近世日本政治史と朝廷』（吉川弘文館、2017年） ISBN 978-4-642-03480-7） 2017年、P229-230。 == 境内 == * 本殿（京都市指定有形文化財） - [[寛政]]3年（[[1791年]]）に仮皇居の[[内侍所]]仮殿を移築したもの。 * 幣殿（京都市指定有形文化財） * 廻廊（京都市指定有形文化財） * 拝所（京都市指定有形文化財） * 拝殿 * 社務所 * 神明社（[[皇大神宮|内宮]]・[[外宮]]） * [[八幡社]] * [[春日社]] - [[文政]]年間（[[1818年]] - [[1831年]]）建立。 * [[猿田彦]]社・垂加社・柿本社 - [[猿田彦]]の他、[[垂加神道]]を唱えた[[山崎闇斎]]と[[柿本人麻呂]]を祀る。 * [[稲荷社]] * [[宗像三女神|宗像社]] * [[天満宮]]社 - 祭神：北野大神 * [[大国主]]社 * 五社相殿社 - 斎部社、[[大将軍]]社、高知穂社、[[愛宕社]]、[[日吉社]] * 神楽所 * 鳳輿舎 * 神門 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財 === * [[霊元天皇]]宸翰御祈願文 === 京都市指定有形文化財 === * 本殿 * 幣殿 * 拝所 * 廻廊 == 祭事 == [[明治]]までは神輿迎が[[7月18日 (旧暦)|7月18日]]に御霊祭は[[8月18日 (旧暦)|8月18日]]に行われていたが、明治以降は5月1日が神幸祭、18日が還幸祭と改められ、8月18日は例祭日と定められて東遊が奉奏される。現在では還幸祭は5月の第3か第4日曜日に行われており、宵宮では地域の小学校と連携した子供神輿も催されている。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[御霊信仰]] * [[上御霊神社]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat| Shimogoryō-jinja}} *[http://shimogoryo.main.jp/ 下御霊神社オフィシャルサイト] {{洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しもこりようしんしや}} [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧府社]] [[Category:単立神社]] [[Category:中京区の歴史]] [[Category:中京区の建築物]] [[Category:洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝]] [[Category:京都府の景観重要建造物]] [[Category:京都市の重要文化財]] [[Category:霊元天皇]]

{{infobox religious building | name = Shimogoryo Shrine | native_name = 下御霊神社 | address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Nakagyo Ward|ja|中京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Teramachi Street|ja|寺町通|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Marutamachi Street|ja|丸太町通|lt=Marutamachi|lt_ja=丸太町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 下る下御霊前町 | coordinates = {{coord|35|01|01|N|135|46|04|E|region:JP-26|type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shimogoryo Shrine}} | deities = 吉備聖霊
{{ill|Prince Hayara|ja|早良親王|lt=Emperor Suidō|lt_ja=崇道天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Fujiwara Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|lt=Lady Fujiwara|lt_ja=藤原大夫人|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Fujiwara Hirotsugu|ja|藤原広嗣|lt=Fujiwara Daitō|lt_ja=藤大夫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

火雷天神 | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|Jōgan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}5th year （{{ill|863|ja|863|qq=draft|qq=draft}}） | status = 旧{{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | pilgrimage = {{ill|Kyoto-Rakuya Hachasha Shūin Meguri|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | festival = 5月第3日曜日（御霊祭） | File = [[File:Shimogoryo_torii.jpg|250px|]] }} '''Shimogoryo Shrine''' (下御霊神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Nakagyo Ward|ja|中京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Shimogoryo‑mae Town. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine’s name corresponds with that of {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == During the era of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, epidemics spread throughout various regions. This was regarded as a curse ({{ill|Curse|ja|祟り|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) stemming from the {{ill|Goryo Faith|ja|御霊信仰|lt=Spirits|lt_ja=御霊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and accordingly, on May 20 of {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|Jōgan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}5 ({{ill|863|ja|863|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), a Goryo Gathering ({{ill|Goryo Gathering|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was held at the {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京|qq=draft|qq=draft}} site of {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This gathering is considered to mark the founding of the enshrinements at both this shrine and {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Since ancient times, it has been revered as the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial Palace|ja|京都御所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Originally, the deity was enshrined in the Goryo Hall of Shimodashimori‑ji (下出雲寺御霊堂) located in the Izumo District of Atago County ({{ill|Atago District|ja|愛宕郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) on the route of {{ill|Izumo Road|ja|出雲路|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. To the north stood {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, known as the Goryo Hall of Kamidashimori‑ji. Later, the shrine was relocated to Shinmachi Izumi, and subsequently, in Tenshō18 ({{ill|1590|ja|1590|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), as part of the urban development initiated by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was moved to the site where {{ill|Saionji Sanjitsu|ja|西園寺実氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} had his villa, Tokiwaido. During the Kyōhō era ({{ill|1716|ja|1716|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – {{ill|1736|ja|1736|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the spirit of {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in Kansei3 ({{ill|1791|ja|1791|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence, after the old building was destroyed in the Great Tenmei Fire of Tenmei8 ({{ill|1788|ja|1788|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was categorized as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Notably, scholar {{ill|Shiga Tsuyoshi|ja|志賀剛|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of Shikinai shrines has identified this shrine with {{ill|Izumo Iokami Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shikinai Taisha) as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} The enshrined deities consist of the following eight gods, collectively known as the "Eight Goryo": * Kibi Seirei (although often identified with {{ill|Kibi Mabibi|ja|吉備真備|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, since Kibi Mabibi did not die in a state of indignation, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|Japanese spirit|ja|和魂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the six divine spirits) * Emperor Suidō (the crown prince of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Prince Hayara|ja|早良親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Lady Fujiwara (mother of Prince Iyo, {{ill|Fujiwara Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Fujiwara Daitō ({{ill|Fujiwara Hirotsugu|ja|藤原広嗣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Tachibana Daifu ({{ill|Tachibana Itsusei|ja|橘逸勢|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Fumi Daifu ({{ill|Fumimuro no Miyata no Maro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|lt=Fumi Daifu|lt_ja=文屋宮田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Karei Tenjin (although often identified with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, since the shrine was founded before he came to be regarded as Tenjin, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|rough spirit|ja|荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the six divine spirits) All these deities are individuals who met their untimely deaths amid political strife, and this shrine was established to appease their vengeful spirits ({{ill|Vengeful Spirit|ja|怨霊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). In the side hall, {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is enshrined as the Tennchūbashira Imperial Deity, which is attributed to the close ties between the shrine’s then priests, the father and son {{ill|Izumori Nobu-nao|ja|出雲路信直|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Izumori Naomoto|ja|出雲路直元|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the emperor. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) – Reconstructed in Kansei3 ({{ill|1791|ja|1791|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence. * Offerings Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Corridor (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Worship Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Prayer Hall * Shrine Office * Shinmeisha ({{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|lt=Naiku|lt_ja=内宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Built during the {{ill|Bunsei era|ja|文政|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1818|ja|1818|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – {{ill|1831|ja|1831|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Sarutahiko Shrine, Tarika Shrine, and Kakimoto Shrine – Enshrining, in addition to {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamazaki Ansai|ja|山崎闇斎|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who advocated {{ill|Tarika Shintō|ja|垂加神道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kakimoto no Hitomaro|ja|柿本人麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Munakata Shrine|lt_ja=宗像社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tenmangu Shrine|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined deity: Kitano Ōkami * {{ill|Ōkuninushi Shrine|ja|大国主|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Goshasōdensho – Saibe Shrine, {{ill|Dai-Shogun Shrine|ja|大将軍|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Shrine, Kochiho Shrine, {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Hiyoshisha|ja|日吉社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Kagura Hall * Palanquin Pavilion * Shrine Gate == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}’s Imperial Edict Prayer Document === Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto City === * Main Hall * Offerings Hall * Worship Hall * Corridor == Festivals == Until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} the Mikoshi Reception was held on {{ill|July 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月18日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Goryo Festival on {{ill|August 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|8月18日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. After the Meiji period, May1 was designated as the Divine Auspice Festival (Shinkō‑sai) and the 18th as the Homecoming Festival (Kankō‑sai), with August18 established as the regular festival day on which a Tōyū performance is offered. Currently, the Homecoming Festival is held on either the third or fourth Sunday of May, and during the evening festival a children’s mikoshi is organized in collaboration with local elementary schools. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Goryo Faith|ja|御霊信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimogoryo-jinja}} * [http://shimogoryo.main.jp/ Shimogoryo Shrine Official Website] {{Kyōyō Tenmangu Twenty-Five Shrine Pilgrimage}} {{Shinto Portal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Independent Shrines]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of the Twenty-Five Shrines of Kyōyō Tenmangu]] [[Category:Important Scenic Buildings of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Emperor Reigen]] [[Category:Translated Shrines and Temples]] [[Category:Shimogoryo Shrine]] [[Category:Goryo Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Shimo-Goryō Shrine]] [[ja:下御霊神社]] {{translated page|ja|下御霊神社|version=101246179|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Originally, the deity was enshrined in the Goryo Hall of Shimodashimori‑ji (下出雲寺御霊堂) located in the Izumo District of Atago County ({{ill|Atago District|ja|愛宕郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) on the route of {{ill|Izumo Road|ja|出雲路|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. To the north stood {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, known as the Goryo Hall of Kamidashimori‑ji. Later, the shrine was relocated to Shinmachi Izumi, and subsequently, in Tenshō18 ({{ill|1590|ja|1590|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), as part of the urban development initiated by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was moved to the site where {{ill|Saionji Sanjitsu|ja|西園寺実氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} had his villa, Tokiwaido. During the Kyōhō era ({{ill|1716|ja|1716|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – {{ill|1736|ja|1736|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the spirit of {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in Kansei3 ({{ill|1791|ja|1791|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence, after the old building was destroyed in the Great Tenmei Fire of Tenmei8 ({{ill|1788|ja|1788|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was categorized as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Notably, scholar {{ill|Shiga Tsuyoshi|ja|志賀剛|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of Shikinai shrines has identified this shrine with {{ill|Izumo Iokami Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shikinai Taisha) as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} The enshrined deities consist of the following eight gods, collectively known as the "Eight Goryo": * Kibi Seirei (although often identified with {{ill|Kibi Mabibi|ja|吉備真備|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, since Kibi Mabibi did not die in a state of indignation, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|Japanese spirit|ja|和魂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the six divine spirits) * Emperor Suidō (the crown prince of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Prince Hayara|ja|早良親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Lady Fujiwara (mother of Prince Iyo, {{ill|Fujiwara Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Fujiwara Daitō ({{ill|Fujiwara Hirotsugu|ja|藤原広嗣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Tachibana Daifu ({{ill|Tachibana Itsusei|ja|橘逸勢|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Fumi Daifu ({{ill|Fumimuro no Miyata no Maro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|lt=Fumi Daifu|lt_ja=文屋宮田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Karei Tenjin (although often identified with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, since the shrine was founded before he came to be regarded as Tenjin, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|rough spirit|ja|荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the six divine spirits) All these deities are individuals who met their untimely deaths amid political strife, and this shrine was established to appease their vengeful spirits ({{ill|Vengeful Spirit|ja|怨霊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). In the side hall, {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is enshrined as the Tennchūbashira Imperial Deity, which is attributed to the close ties between the shrine’s then priests, the father and son {{ill|Izumori Nobu-nao|ja|出雲路信直|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Izumori Naomoto|ja|出雲路直元|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the emperor. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) – Reconstructed in Kansei3 ({{ill|1791|ja|1791|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence. * Offerings Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Corridor (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Worship Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Prayer Hall * Shrine Office * Shinmeisha ({{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|lt=Naiku|lt_ja=内宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Built during the {{ill|Bunsei era|ja|文政|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1818|ja|1818|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – {{ill|1831|ja|1831|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Sarutahiko Shrine, Tarika Shrine, and Kakimoto Shrine – Enshrining, in addition to {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamazaki Ansai|ja|山崎闇斎|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who advocated {{ill|Tarika Shintō|ja|垂加神道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kakimoto no Hitomaro|ja|柿本人麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Munakata Shrine|lt_ja=宗像社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tenmangu Shrine|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined deity: Kitano Ōkami * {{ill|Ōkuninushi Shrine|ja|大国主|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Goshasōdensho – Saibe Shrine, {{ill|Dai-Shogun Shrine|ja|大将軍|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Shrine, Kochiho Shrine, {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Hiyoshisha|ja|日吉社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Kagura Hall * Palanquin Pavilion * Shrine Gate == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}’s Imperial Edict Prayer Document === Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto City === * Main Hall * Offerings Hall * Worship Hall * Corridor == Festivals == Until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} the Mikoshi Reception was held on {{ill|July 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月18日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Goryo Festival on {{ill|August 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|8月18日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. After the Meiji period, May1 was designated as the Divine Auspice Festival (Shinkō‑sai) and the 18th as the Homecoming Festival (Kankō‑sai), with August18 established as the regular festival day on which a Tōyū performance is offered. Currently, the Homecoming Festival is held on either the third or fourth Sunday of May, and during the evening festival a children’s mikoshi is organized in collaboration with local elementary schools. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Goryo Faith|ja|御霊信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimogoryo-jinja}} * [http://shimogoryo.main.jp/ Shimogoryo Shrine Official Website] {{Kyōyō Tenmangu Twenty-Five Shrine Pilgrimage}} {{Shinto Portal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|下御霊神社|version=101246179|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Independent Shrines]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of the Twenty-Five Shrines of Kyōyō Tenmangu]] [[Category:Important Scenic Buildings of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Emperor Reigen]] [[Category:Translated Shrines and Temples]] [[Category:Shimogoryo Shrine]] [[Category:Goryo Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Shimo-Goryō Shrine]] [[ja:下御霊神社]]

{{infobox religious building | name = Shimogoryo Shrine | native_name = 下御霊神社 | address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Nakagyo Ward|ja|中京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Teramachi Street|ja|寺町通|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Marutamachi Street|ja|丸太町通|lt=Marutamachi|lt_ja=丸太町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 下る下御霊前町 | coordinates = {{coord|35|01|01|N|135|46|04|E|region:JP-26|type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shimogoryo Shrine}} | deities = 吉備聖霊

{{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Fujiwara Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|lt=Lady Fujiwara|lt_ja=藤原大夫人|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

火雷天神 | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|Jōgan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}5th year （{{ill|863|ja|863|qq=draft|qq=draft}}） | status = 旧{{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | pilgrimage = {{ill|Kyoto-Rakuya Hachasha Shūin Meguri|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | festival = 5月第3日曜日（御霊祭） | File = [[File:Shimogoryo_torii.jpg|250px|]] }} '''Shimogoryo Shrine''' (下御霊神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Nakagyo Ward|ja|中京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Shimogoryo‑mae Town. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine’s name corresponds with that of {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == During the era of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, epidemics spread throughout various regions. This was regarded as a curse ({{ill|Curse|ja|祟り|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) stemming from the {{ill|Goryo Faith|ja|御霊信仰|lt=Spirits|lt_ja=御霊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and accordingly, on May 20 of {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|Jōgan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}5 ({{ill|863|ja|863|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), a Goryo Gathering ({{ill|Goryo Gathering|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was held at the {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京|qq=draft|qq=draft}} site of {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This gathering is considered to mark the founding of the enshrinements at both this shrine and {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Since ancient times, it has been revered as the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial Palace|ja|京都御所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Originally, the deity was enshrined in the Goryo Hall of Shimodashimori‑ji (下出雲寺御霊堂) located in the Izumo District of Atago County ({{ill|Atago District|ja|愛宕郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) on the route of {{ill|Izumo Road|ja|出雲路|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. To the north stood {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, known as the Goryo Hall of Kamidashimori‑ji. Later, the shrine was relocated to Shinmachi Izumi, and subsequently, in Tenshō18 ({{ill|1590|ja|1590|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), as part of the urban development initiated by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was moved to the site where {{ill|Saionji Sanjitsu|ja|西園寺実氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} had his villa, Tokiwaido. During the Kyōhō era ({{ill|1716|ja|1716|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – {{ill|1736|ja|1736|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the spirit of {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in Kansei3 ({{ill|1791|ja|1791|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence, after the old building was destroyed in the Great Tenmei Fire of Tenmei8 ({{ill|1788|ja|1788|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was categorized as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Notably, scholar {{ill|Shiga Tsuyoshi|ja|志賀剛|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of Shikinaisha has identified this shrine with {{ill|Izumo Iokami Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shikinai Taisha) as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} The enshrined deities consist of the following eight gods, collectively known as the "Eight Goryo": * Kibi Seirei (although often identified with {{ill|Kibi Mabibi|ja|吉備真備|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, since Kibi Mabibi did not die in a state of indignation, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|Japanese spirit|ja|和魂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the six divine spirits) * Emperor Suidō (the crown prince of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Prince Hayara|ja|早良親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Lady Fujiwara (mother of Prince Iyo, {{ill|Fujiwara Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Fujiwara Daitō ({{ill|Fujiwara Hirotsugu|ja|藤原広嗣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Tachibana Daifu ({{ill|Tachibana Itsusei|ja|橘逸勢|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Fumi Daifu ({{ill|Fumimuro no Miyata no Maro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|lt=Fumi Daifu|lt_ja=文屋宮田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Karei Tenjin (although often identified with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, since the shrine was founded before he came to be regarded as Tenjin, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|rough spirit|ja|荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the six divine spirits) All these deities are individuals who met their untimely deaths amid political strife, and this shrine was established to appease their vengeful spirits ({{ill|Vengeful Spirit|ja|怨霊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). In the side hall, {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is enshrined as the Tennchūbashira Imperial Deity, which is attributed to the close ties between the shrine’s then priests, the father and son {{ill|Izumori Nobu-nao|ja|出雲路信直|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Izumori Naomoto|ja|出雲路直元|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the emperor. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall (a designated tangible Kyoto City) * Prayer Hall * Shrine Office * Shinmeisha ({{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|lt=Naiku|lt_ja=内宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Hachiman 火雷天神 | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|Jōgan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}5th year （{{ill|863|ja|863|qq=draft|qq=draft}}） | status = 旧{{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | pilgrimage = {{ill|Kyoto-Rakuya Hachasha Shūin Meguri|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | festival = 5月第3日曜日（御霊祭） | File = [[File:Shimogoryo_torii.jpg|250px|]] }} '''Shimogoryo Shrine''' (下御霊神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Nakagyo Ward|ja|中京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Shimogoryo‑mae Town. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine’s name corresponds with that of {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == During the era of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, epidemics spread throughout various regions. This was regarded as a curse ({{ill|Curse|ja|祟り|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) stemming from the {{ill|Goryo Faith|ja|御霊信仰|lt=Spirits|lt_ja=御霊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and accordingly, on May 20 of {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|Jōgan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}5 ({{ill|863|ja|863|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), a Goryo Gathering ({{ill|Goryo Gathering|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was held at the {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京|qq=draft|qq=draft}} site of {{ill|Shinsen-en|ja|神泉苑|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This gathering is considered to mark the founding of the enshrinements at both this shrine and {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Since ancient times, it has been revered as the tutelary deity of the {{ill|Kyoto Imperial Palace|ja|京都御所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Originally, the deity was enshrined in the Goryo Hall of Shimodashimori‑ji (下出雲寺御霊堂) located in the Izumo District of Atago County ({{ill|Atago District|ja|愛宕郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) on the route of {{ill|Izumo Road|ja|出雲路|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. To the north stood {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, known as the Goryo Hall of Kamidashimori‑ji. Later, the shrine was relocated to Shinmachi Izumi, and subsequently, in Tenshō18 ({{ill|1590|ja|1590|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), as part of the urban development initiated by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was moved to the site where {{ill|Saionji Sanjitsu|ja|西園寺実氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} had his villa, Tokiwaido. During the Kyōhō era ({{ill|1716|ja|1716|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – {{ill|1736|ja|1736|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the spirit of {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in Kansei3 ({{ill|1791|ja|1791|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence, after the old building was destroyed in the Great Tenmei Fire of Tenmei8 ({{ill|1788|ja|1788|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was categorized as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Notably, scholar {{ill|Shiga Tsuyoshi|ja|志賀剛|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of Shikinaisha has identified this shrine with {{ill|Izumo Iokami Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shikinai Taisha) as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} The enshrined deities consist of the following eight gods, collectively known as the "Eight Goryo": * Kibi Seirei (although often identified with {{ill|Kibi Mabibi|ja|吉備真備|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, since Kibi Mabibi did not die in a state of indignation, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|Japanese spirit|ja|和魂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the six divine spirits) * Emperor Suidō (the crown prince of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Prince Hayara|ja|早良親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Lady Fujiwara (mother of Prince Iyo, {{ill|Fujiwara Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Fujiwara Daitō ({{ill|Fujiwara Hirotsugu|ja|藤原広嗣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Tachibana Daifu ({{ill|Tachibana Itsusei|ja|橘逸勢|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Fumi Daifu ({{ill|Fumimuro no Miyata no Maro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|lt=Fumi Daifu|lt_ja=文屋宮田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Karei Tenjin (although often identified with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, since the shrine was founded before he came to be regarded as Tenjin, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|rough spirit|ja|荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the six divine spirits) All these deities are individuals who met their untimely deaths amid political strife, and this shrine was established to appease their vengeful spirits ({{ill|Vengeful Spirit|ja|怨霊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). In the side hall, {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is enshrined as the Tennchūbashira Imperial Deity, which is attributed to the close ties between the shrine’s then priests, the father and son {{ill|Izumori Nobu-nao|ja|出雲路信直|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Izumori Naomoto|ja|出雲路直元|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the emperor. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) – Reconstructed in Kansei3 ({{ill|1791|ja|1791|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence. * Offerings Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Corridor (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Worship Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Prayer Hall * Shrine Office * Shinmeisha ({{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|lt=Naiku|lt_ja=内宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Built during the {{ill|Bunsei era|ja|文政|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1818|ja|1818|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – {{ill|1831|ja|1831|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Sarutahiko Shrine, Tarika Shrine, and Kakimoto Shrine – Enshrining, in addition to {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamazaki Ansai|ja|山崎闇斎|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who advocated {{ill|Tarika Shintō|ja|垂加神道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kakimoto no Hitomaro|ja|柿本人麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Munakata Shrine|lt_ja=宗像社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tenmangu Shrine|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Enshrined deity: Kitano Ōkami * {{ill|Ōkuninushi Shrine|ja|大国主|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Goshasōdensho – Saibe Shrine, {{ill|Dai-Shogun Shrine|ja|大将軍|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Shrine, Kochiho Shrine, {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Hiyoshisha|ja|日吉社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Kagura Hall * Palanquin Pavilion * Shrine Gate == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}’s Imperial Edict Prayer Document === Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto City === * Main Hall * Offerings Hall * Worship Hall * Corridor == Festivals == Until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} the Mikoshi Reception was held on {{ill|July 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月18日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Goryo Festival on {{ill|August 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|8月18日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. After the Meiji period, May1 was designated as the Divine Auspice Festival (Shinkō‑sai) and the 18th as the Homecoming Festival (Kankō‑sai), with August18 established as the regular festival day on which a Tōyū performance is offered. Currently, the Homecoming Festival is held on either the third or fourth Sunday of May, and during the evening festival a children’s mikoshi is organized in collaboration with local elementary schools. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Goryo Faith|ja|御霊信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimogoryo-jinja}} * [http://shimogoryo.main.jp/ Shimogoryo Shrine Official Website] {{Kyōyō Tenmangu Twenty-Five Shrine Pilgrimage}} {{Shinto Portal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|下御霊神社|version=101246179|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] [[Category:Independent Shrines]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of the Twenty-Five Shrines of Kyōyō Tenmangu]] [[Category:Important Scenic Buildings of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Emperor Reigen]] [[Category:Translated Shrines and Temples]] [[Category:Shimogoryo Shrine]] [[Category:Goryo Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Shimo-Goryō Shrine]] [[ja:下御霊神社]]

Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Emperor Reigen|ja|霊元天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Reigen|qq=}}’s Imperial Edict Prayer Document === Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto City === * Main Hall * Offerings Hall * Worship Hall * Corridor == Festivals == Until the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} the Mikoshi Reception was held on {{ill|July 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月18日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 18 (lunar calendar)|qq=}} and the Goryo Festival on {{ill|August 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|8月18日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 18 (lunar calendar)|qq=}}. After the Meiji period, May1 was designated as the Divine Auspice Festival (Shinkō‑sai) and the 18th as the Homecoming Festival (Kankō‑sai), with August18 established as the regular festival day on which a Tōyū performance is offered. Currently, the Homecoming Festival is held on either the third or fourth Sunday of May, and during the evening festival a children’s mikoshi is organized in collaboration with local elementary schools. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Goryo Faith|ja|御霊信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryo Faith|qq=}} * {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Goryo Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimogoryo-jinja}} * [http://shimogoryo.main.jp/ Shimogoryo Shrine Official Website] {{Kyōyō Tenmangu Twenty-Five Shrine Pilgrimage}} {{Shinto Portal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|下御霊神社|version=101246179|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] [[Category:Independent Shrines]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo Ward]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of the Twenty-Five Shrines of Kyōyō Tenmangu]] [[Category:Important Scenic Buildings of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kyoto City]] [[Category:Emperor Reigen]] [[Category:Translated Shrines and Temples]] [[Category:Shimogoryo Shrine]] [[Category:Goryo Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Shimo-Goryō Shrine]] [[ja:下御霊神社]]

{{ill|Prince Hayara|ja|早良親王|lt=Emperor Suidō|lt_ja=崇道天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Hayara|qq=}}
