火雷天神 | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|Jōgan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan era|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}5th year （[[:en:863|863]]） | status = 旧{{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fu-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | pilgrimage = {{ill|Kyoto-Rakuya Hachasha Shūin Meguri|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto-Rakuya Hachasha Shūin Meguri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | festival = 5月第3日曜日（御霊祭） | File = [[File:Shimogoryo_torii.jpg|250px|]] | image = File:Shimogoryo torii.jpg | location = {{ill|Nakagyō Ward|WD=Q1075455}} | deity = {{ill|Fujiwara no Hirotsugu|WD=Q3090626}}; {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|WD=Q11623103}}; {{ill|Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|WD=Q11499944}}; {{ill|Iyo-shinnō|WD=Q11378831}}; {{ill|Kibi-naishinnō|WD=Q11412639}}; {{ill|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634}}; {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|WD=Q1062686}} }} '''Shimogoryo Shrine''' (下御霊神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]][[:en:Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|Nakagyo Ward]], Shimogoryo‑mae Town. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fu-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine’s name corresponds with that of {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Goryo Shrine|qq=|1=Kamigoryō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359018}}. == History == During the era of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]], epidemics spread throughout various regions. This was regarded as a curse ({{ill|Curse|ja|祟り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Curse|qq=|1=tatari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109944776}}) stemming from the [[:en:Goryō|Spirits]], and accordingly, on May 20 of {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|Jōgan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan era|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}5 ([[:en:863|863]]), a Goryo Gathering ({{ill|Goryo Gathering|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryo Gathering|qq=|1=goryō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489354}}) was held at the [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]] site of [[:en:Shinsenen|Shinsen-en]]. This gathering is considered to mark the founding of the enshrinements at both this shrine and {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Goryo Shrine|qq=|1=Kamigoryō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359018}}. Since ancient times, it has been revered as the tutelary deity of the [[:en:Kyoto Imperial Palace|Kyoto Imperial Palace]]. Originally, the deity was enshrined in the Goryo Hall of Shimodashimori‑ji (下出雲寺御霊堂) located in the Izumo District of Atago County ({{ill|Atago District|ja|愛宕郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago District|qq=|1=Otagi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10981345}}, {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo District|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) on the route of {{ill|Izumo Road|ja|出雲路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Road|qq=|1=Izumoji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395906}}. To the north stood {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Goryo Shrine|qq=|1=Kamigoryō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359018}}, known as the Goryo Hall of Kamidashimori‑ji. Later, the shrine was relocated to Shinmachi Izumi, and subsequently, in Tenshō18 ([[:en:1590|1590]]), as part of the urban development initiated by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], it was moved to the site where [[:en:Saionji Saneuji|Saionji Sanjitsu]] had his villa, Tokiwaido. During the Kyōhō era ([[:en:1716|1716]] – [[:en:1736|1736]]), the spirit of [[:en:Emperor Reigen|Emperor Reigen]] was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in Kansei3 ([[:en:1791|1791]]) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence, after the old building was destroyed in the Great Tenmei Fire of Tenmei8 ([[:en:1788|1788]]). In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was categorized as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fu-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Notably, scholar {{ill|Shiga Tsuyoshi|ja|志賀剛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Tsuyoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Shikinaisha has identified this shrine with {{ill|Izumo Iokami Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Iokami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shikinai Taisha) as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The enshrined deities consist of the following eight gods, collectively known as the "Eight Goryo": * Kibi Seirei (although often identified with [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi Mabibi]], since Kibi Mabibi did not die in a state of indignation, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|Japanese spirit|ja|和魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese spirit|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}} of the six divine spirits) * Emperor Suidō (the crown prince of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]], [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Hayara]]) * {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}} (son of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]]) * Lady Fujiwara (mother of Prince Iyo, {{ill|Fujiwara Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Yoshiko|qq=|1=Fujiwara no Yoshiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623103}}) * Fujiwara Daitō ({{ill|Fujiwara ([[:en:Tachibana no Hayanari|Tachibana Itsusei]]) * Fumi Daifu ({{ill|Fumimuro no Miyata no Maro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|lt=Fumi Daifu|lt_ja=文屋宮田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fumimuro no Miyata no Maro|qq=|1=Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499944}}) * Karei Tenjin (although often identified with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, since the shrine was founded before he came to be regarded as Tenjin, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|rough spirit|ja|荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rough spirit|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}} of the six divine spirits) All these deities are individuals who met their untimely deaths amid political strife, and this shrine was established to appease their vengeful spirits ([[:en:Onryō|Vengeful Spirit]]). In the side hall, [[:en:Emperor Reigen|Emperor Reigen]] is enshrined as the Tennchūbashira Imperial Deity, which is attributed to the close ties between the shrine’s then priests, the father and son {{ill|Izumori Nobu-nao|ja|出雲路信直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumori Nobu-nao|qq=|1=Izumoji Nobunao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395908}} and {{ill|Izumori Naomoto|ja|出雲路直元|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumori Naomoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the emperor. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall (a designated tangible cultural Hall * Palanquin Pavilion * Shrine Gate == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * [[:en:Emperor Reigen|Emperor Reigen]]’s Imperial Edict Prayer Document === Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto City === * Main Hall * Offerings Hall * Worship Hall * Corridor == Festivals == Until the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] the Mikoshi Reception was held on {{ill|July 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月18日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 18 (lunar calendar)|qq=|1=18th day of the 7th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839924}} and the Goryo Festival on {{ill|August 18 (Lunisolar)|ja|8月18日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 18 (lunar calendar)|qq=}}. After the Meiji period, May1 was designated as the Divine Auspice Festival (Shinkō‑sai) and the 18th as the Homecoming Festival (Kankō‑sai), with August18 established as the regular festival day on which a Tōyū performance is offered. Currently, the Homecoming Festival is held on either the third or fourth Sunday of May, and during the evening festival a children’s mikoshi is organized in collaboration with local elementary schools. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Goryō|Goryo Faith]] * {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Goryo Shrine|qq=|1=Kamigoryō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359018}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimogoryo-jinja}} * [http://shimogoryo.main.jp/ Shimogoryo Shrine Official Website] {{Kyōyō Tenmangu Twenty-Five Shrine Pilgrimage}} {{Shinto Portal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|下御霊神社|version=101246179|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] [[Category:Emperor Reigen]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:下御霊神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shimo-Goryō Shrine |location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto]], Nakagyō Ward, Teramachi-dōri Marutamachi-sagaru Shimogoryōmae-chō |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|01|01|N|135|46|04|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shimo-Goryō Shrine}} |deity = Yoshitsugu Seiō

Kazan Tenjin |consecration_year = [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] 5 (863 CE) |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Festival = 3rd Sunday of May (Goryō Festival) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Shimogoryo_torii.jpg|250px|]] }} '''Shimo-Goryō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|下御霊神社}}, Shimogoryō Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|Nakagyō-ku]], Kyoto City. Its name corresponds to {{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}, reflecting its complementary role. The shrine is historically regarded as one of the key sites of [[:en:Goryō|goryō beliefs]], dedicated to appeasing vengeful spirits. == History == The shrine traces its origins to the [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 5 (863 CE) {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|qq=|1=goryō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489354}}, a ritual held at the [[:en:Shinsenen|Shinsen-en]] in Kyoto to appease the spirits of those who had died tragically. The epidemic plaguing the land at the time was believed to be caused by these restless spirits. Shimo-Goryō Shrine and its counterpart Kamigoryō Shrine were both established following this ceremony. Originally, Shimo-Goryō Shrine was located in Izumojizō in the {{ill|Atago District|ja|愛宕郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago District|qq=|1=Otagi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10981345}} but was moved to Shinmachi Demizu. In [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 18 (1590 CE), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] relocated the shrine to its current location, the former estate of [[:en:Saionji Saneuji|Saionji Sanemitsu]]. The shrine's main hall dates back to [[:en:Kansei|Kansei]] 3 (1791 CE), after the original structure was destroyed in the {{ill|Great Tenmei Fire|ja|天明の大火|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Tenmei Fire|qq=|1=Great Fire of Tenmei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442674}}. The current hall was repurposed from the temporary inner sanctum of the Imperial Palace. During the [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] era (1716–1736), [[:en:Emperor Reigen|Emperor Reigen]]'s spirit was enshrined as a secondary deity, reflecting the close ties between the shrine's priests and the emperor. == Deities == Shimo-Goryō Shrine enshrines eight primary deities, collectively known as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Yoshitsugu Seiō (interpreted as the harmonious spirit of the six deities) * {{ill|Sudō Tennō|ja|崇道天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 早良親王|1=Prince Sawara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1079634}} (Prince Sawara, son of Emperor Kanmu) * {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}} (son of Emperor Kanmu) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|qq=|1=Fujiwara no Yoshiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623103}} (mother of Prince Iyo) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Hirotsugu|ja|藤原広嗣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Hirotsugu|qq=|1=Fujiwara no Hirotsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090626}} * [[:en:Tachibana no Hayanari|Tachibana no Hayanari]] * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|qq=|1=Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499944}} * Kazan Tenjin (interpreted as the wrathful spirit of the six deities) Additionally, Emperor Reigen is enshrined in the main hall's auxiliary sanctum. These deities represent figures who suffered tragic or unjust deaths, and the shrine was founded to appease their spirits and prevent their wrath from causing harm. == Cultural Assets == === Important Cultural Property === * A prayer document written by [[:en:Emperor Reigen|Emperor Reigen]] === Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Main hall * Hei-den (offering hall) * Covered corridors * Worship hall == Festivals == The shrine's annual {{ill|Goryō Festival|ja|御霊祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is one of its most significant events. Traditionally held on [[:en:July 18|July 18 (old calendar)]] and [[:en:August 18|August 18 (old calendar)]], the dates were adjusted in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] to May 1 for the Shinkōsai (portable shrine procession) and May 18 for the Kangyōsai (return procession). The modern festival is celebrated on the third or fourth Sunday of May, with events such as children's mikoshi parades organized in collaboration with local schools. == External Links == * [http://shimogoryo.main.jp/ Official Website] * {{Commonscat|Shimo-Goryō Shrine}} {{洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝}} {{translated page|ja|下御霊神社|version=101246179|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:下御霊神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Important Scenic Structures in Kyōto Prefecture]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of 25 Shrines of Rakuyō Tenmangū]] [[Category:Emperor Reigen]] {{Commons category|Shimogoryō-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11360904}}

火雷天神 | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|Jōgan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}5th year （[[:en:863|863]]） | status = 旧{{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fu-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | pilgrimage = {{ill|Kyoto-Rakuya Hachasha Shūin Meguri|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto-Rakuya Hachasha Shūin Meguri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | festival = 5月第3日曜日（御霊祭） | File = [[File:Shimogoryo_torii.jpg|250px|]] | image = File:Shimogoryo torii.jpg | location = {{ill|Nakagyō Ward|WD=Q1075455}} | deity = {{ill|Fujiwara no Hirotsugu|WD=Q3090626}}; {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|WD=Q11623103}}; {{ill|Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|WD=Q11499944}}; {{ill|Iyo-shinnō|WD=Q11378831}}; {{ill|Kibi-naishinnō|WD=Q11412639}}; {{ill|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634}}; {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|WD=Q1062686}} }} '''Shimogoryo Shrine''' (下御霊神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]][[:en:Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|Nakagyo Ward]], Shimogoryo‑mae Town. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fu-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine’s name corresponds with that of {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Goryo Shrine|1=Kamigoryō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359018}}. == History == During the era of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]], epidemics spread throughout various regions. This was regarded as a curse ({{ill|Curse|ja|祟り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Curse|1=tatari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109944776}}) stemming from the [[:en:Goryō|Spirits]], and accordingly, on May 20 of {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|Jōgan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}5 ([[:en:863|863]]), a Goryo Gathering ({{ill|Goryo Gathering|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryo Gathering|1=goryō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489354}}) was held at the [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]] site of [[:en:Shinsenen|Shinsen-en]]. This gathering is considered to mark the founding of the enshrinements at both this shrine and {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Goryo Shrine|1=Kamigoryō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359018}}. Since ancient times, it has been revered as the tutelary deity of the [[:en:Kyoto Imperial Palace|Kyoto Imperial Palace]]. Originally, the deity was enshrined in the Goryo Hall of Shimodashimori‑ji (下出雲寺御霊堂) located in the Izumo District of Atago County ({{ill|Atago District|ja|愛宕郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago District|1=Otagi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10981345}}, {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) on the route of {{ill|Izumo Road|ja|出雲路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Road|1=Izumoji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395906}}. To the north stood {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Goryo Shrine|1=Kamigoryō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359018}}, known as the Goryo Hall of Kamidashimori‑ji. Later, the shrine was relocated to Shinmachi Izumi, and subsequently, in Tenshō18 ([[:en:1590|1590]]), as part of the urban development initiated by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], it was moved to the site where [[:en:Saionji Saneuji|Saionji Sanjitsu]] had his villa, Tokiwaido. During the Kyōhō era ([[:en:1716|1716]] – [[:en:1736|1736]]), the spirit of [[:en:Emperor Reigen|Emperor Reigen]] was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in Kansei3 ([[:en:1791|1791]]) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence, after the old building was destroyed in the Great Tenmei Fire of Tenmei8 ([[:en:1788|1788]]). In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was categorized as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fu-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Notably, scholar {{ill|Shiga Tsuyoshi|ja|志賀剛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Tsuyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Shikinaisha has identified this shrine with {{ill|Izumo Iokami Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Iokami Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shikinai Taisha) as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The enshrined deities consist of the following eight gods, collectively known as the "Eight Goryo": * Kibi Seirei (although often identified with [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi Mabibi]], since Kibi Mabibi did not die in a state of indignation, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|Japanese spirit|ja|和魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese spirit|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}} of the six divine spirits) * Emperor Suidō (the crown prince of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]], [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Hayara]]) * {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}} (son of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]]) * Lady Fujiwara (mother of Prince Iyo, {{ill|Fujiwara Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Yoshiko|1=Fujiwara no Yoshiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623103}}) * Fujiwara Daitō ({{ill|Fujiwara Hirotsugu|ja|藤原広嗣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hirotsugu|1=Fujiwara no Hirotsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090626}}) * Tachibana Daifu ([[:en:Tachibana no Hayanari|Tachibana Itsusei]]) * Fumi Daifu ({{ill|Fumimuro no Miyata no Maro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|lt=Fumi Daifu|lt_ja=文屋宮田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fumimuro no Miyata no Maro|1=Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499944}}) * Karei Tenjin (although often identified with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, since the shrine was founded before he came to be regarded as Tenjin, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|rough spirit|ja|荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rough spirit|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}} of the six divine spirits) All these deities are individuals who met their untimely deaths amid political strife, and this shrine was established to appease their vengeful spirits ([[:en:Onryō|Vengeful Spirit]]). In the side hall, [[:en:Emperor Reigen|Emperor Reigen]] is enshrined as the Tennchūbashira Imperial Deity, which is attributed to the close ties between the shrine’s then priests, the father and son {{ill|Izumori Nobu-nao|ja|出雲路信直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumori Nobu-nao|1=Izumoji Nobunao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395908}} and {{ill|Izumori Naomoto|ja|出雲路直元|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumori Naomoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the emperor. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) – Reconstructed in Kansei3 ([[:en:1791|1791]]) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence. * Offerings Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Corridor (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Worship Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Prayer Hall * Shrine Office * Shinmeisha ({{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|lt=Naiku|lt_ja=内宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Hitomaro]] * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神社|1=Inari shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q514480}} * {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Munakata Shrine|lt_ja=宗像社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Shrine|ja|大将軍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai-Shogun Shrine|1=taishōgun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5369529}} Shrine, Kochiho Shrine, {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 愛宕権現}}, and Hall * Palanquin Pavilion * Shrine Gate == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * [[:en:Emperor Reigen|Emperor Reigen]]’s Imperial Edict Prayer Document === Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto City === * Main Hall * Offerings Hall * Worship Hall * Corridor == Festivals == Until the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] the Mikoshi Reception was held on {{ill|July 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月18日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 18 (lunar calendar)|1=18th day of the 7th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839924}} and the Goryo Festival on {{ill|August 18 (Lunisolar)|ja|8月18日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 18 (lunar calendar)|qq=}}. After the Meiji period, May1 was designated as the Divine Auspice Festival (Shinkō‑sai) and the 18th as the Homecoming Festival (Kankō‑sai), with August18 established as the regular festival day on which a Tōyū performance is offered. Currently, the Homecoming Festival is held on either the third or fourth Sunday of May, and during the evening festival a children’s mikoshi is organized in collaboration with local elementary schools. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Goryō|Goryo Faith]] * {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Goryo Shrine|1=Kamigoryō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359018}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimogoryo-jinja}} * [http://shimogoryo.main.jp/ Shimogoryo Shrine Official Website] {{Kyōyō Tenmangu Twenty-Five Shrine Pilgrimage}} {{Shinto Portal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|下御霊神社|version=101246179|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] [[Category:Emperor Reigen]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:下御霊神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shimo-Goryō Shrine |location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto]], Nakagyō Ward, Teramachi-dōri Marutamachi-sagaru Shimogoryōmae-chō |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|01|01|N|135|46|04|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shimo-Goryō Shrine}} |deity = Yoshitsugu Seiō

Kazan Tenjin |consecration_year = [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] 5 (863 CE) |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Festival = 3rd Sunday of May (Goryō Festival) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Shimogoryo_torii.jpg|250px|]] }} '''Shimo-Goryō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|下御霊神社}}, Shimogoryō Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|Nakagyō-ku]], Kyoto City. Its name corresponds to {{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}, reflecting its complementary role. The shrine is historically regarded as one of the key sites of [[:en:Goryō|goryō beliefs]], dedicated to appeasing vengeful spirits. == History == The shrine traces its origins to the [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 5 (863 CE) {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|1=goryō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489354}}, a ritual held at the [[:en:Shinsenen|Shinsen-en]] in Kyoto to appease the spirits of those who had died tragically. The epidemic plaguing the land at the time was believed to be caused by these restless spirits. Shimo-Goryō Shrine and its counterpart Kamigoryō Shrine were both established following this ceremony. Originally, Shimo-Goryō Shrine was located in Izumojizō in the {{ill|Atago District|ja|愛宕郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago District|1=Otagi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10981345}} but was moved to Shinmachi Demizu. In [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 18 (1590 CE), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] relocated the shrine to its current location, the former estate of [[:en:Saionji Saneuji|Saionji Sanemitsu]]. The shrine's main hall dates back to [[:en:Kansei|Kansei]] 3 (1791 CE), after the original structure was destroyed in the {{ill|Great Tenmei Fire|ja|天明の大火|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Tenmei Fire|1=Great Fire of Tenmei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442674}}. The current hall was repurposed from the temporary inner sanctum of the Imperial Palace. During the [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] era (1716–1736), [[:en:Emperor Reigen|Emperor Reigen]]'s spirit was enshrined as a secondary deity, reflecting the close ties between the shrine's priests and the emperor. == Deities == Shimo-Goryō Shrine enshrines eight primary deities, collectively known as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Yoshitsugu Seiō (interpreted as the harmonious spirit of the six deities) * {{ill|Sudō Tennō|ja|崇道天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 早良親王|1=Prince Sawara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1079634}} (Prince Sawara, son of Emperor Kanmu) * {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}} (son of Emperor Kanmu) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|1=Fujiwara no Yoshiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623103}} (mother of Prince Iyo) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Hirotsugu|ja|藤原広嗣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Hirotsugu|1=Fujiwara no Hirotsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090626}} * [[:en:Tachibana no Hayanari|Tachibana no Hayanari]] * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|1=Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499944}} * Kazan Tenjin (interpreted as the wrathful spirit of the six deities) Additionally, Emperor Reigen is enshrined in the main hall's auxiliary sanctum. These deities represent figures who suffered tragic or unjust deaths, and the shrine was founded to appease their spirits and prevent their wrath from causing harm. == Cultural Assets == === Important Cultural Property === * A prayer document written by [[:en:Emperor Reigen|Emperor Reigen]] === Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Main hall * Hei-den (offering hall) * Covered corridors * Worship hall == Festivals == The shrine's annual {{ill|Goryō Festival|ja|御霊祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is one of its most significant events. Traditionally held on [[:en:July 18|July 18 (old calendar)]] and [[:en:August 18|August 18 (old calendar)]], the dates were adjusted in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] to May 1 for the Shinkōsai (portable shrine procession) and May 18 for the Kangyōsai (return procession). The modern festival is celebrated on the third or fourth Sunday of May, with events such as children's mikoshi parades organized in collaboration with local schools. == External Links == * [http://shimogoryo.main.jp/ Official Website] * {{Commonscat|Shimo-Goryō Shrine}} {{洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝}} {{translated page|ja|下御霊神社|version=101246179|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:下御霊神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Important Scenic Structures in Kyōto Prefecture]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of 25 Shrines of Rakuyō Tenmangū]] [[Category:Emperor Reigen]] {{Commons category|Shimogoryō-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11360904}}

火雷天神 | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|Jōgan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}5th year （[[:en:863|863]]） | status = 旧{{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fu-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | pilgrimage = {{ill|Kyoto-Rakuya Hachasha Shūin Meguri|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto-Rakuya Hachasha Shūin Meguri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | festival = 5月第3日曜日（御霊祭） | File = [[File:Shimogoryo_torii.jpg|250px|]] | image = File:Shimogoryo torii.jpg | location = {{ill|Nakagyō Ward|WD=Q1075455}} | deity = {{ill|Fujiwara no Hirotsugu|WD=Q3090626}}; {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|WD=Q11623103}}; {{ill|Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|WD=Q11499944}}; {{ill|Iyo-shinnō|WD=Q11378831}}; {{ill|Kibi-naishinnō|WD=Q11412639}}; {{ill|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634}}; {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|WD=Q1062686}} }} '''Shimogoryo Shrine''' (下御霊神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]][[:en:Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|Nakagyo Ward]], Shimogoryo‑mae Town. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fu-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine’s name corresponds with that of {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Goryo Shrine|1=Kamigoryō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359018}}. == History == During the era of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]], epidemics spread throughout various regions. This was regarded as a curse ({{ill|Curse|ja|祟り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Curse|1=tatari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109944776}}) stemming from the [[:en:Goryō|Spirits]], and accordingly, on May 20 of {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|Jōgan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}5 ([[:en:863|863]]), a Goryo Gathering ({{ill|Goryo Gathering|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryo Gathering|1=goryō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489354}}) was held at the [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]] site of [[:en:Shinsenen|Shinsen-en]]. This gathering is considered to mark the founding of the enshrinements at both this shrine and {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Goryo Shrine|1=Kamigoryō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359018}}. Since ancient times, it has been revered as the tutelary deity of the [[:en:Kyoto Imperial Palace|Kyoto Imperial Palace]]. Originally, the deity was enshrined in the Goryo Hall of Shimodashimori‑ji (下出雲寺御霊堂) located in the Izumo District of Atago County ({{ill|Atago District|ja|愛宕郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago District|1=Otagi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10981345}}, {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) on the route of {{ill|Izumo Road|ja|出雲路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Road|1=Izumoji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395906}}. To the north stood {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Goryo Shrine|1=Kamigoryō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359018}}, known as the Goryo Hall of Kamidashimori‑ji. Later, the shrine was relocated to Shinmachi Izumi, and subsequently, in Tenshō18 ([[:en:1590|1590]]), as part of the urban development initiated by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], it was moved to the site where [[:en:Saionji Saneuji|Saionji Sanjitsu]] had his villa, Tokiwaido. During the Kyōhō era ([[:en:1716|1716]] – [[:en:1736|1736]]), the spirit of [[:en:Emperor Reigen|Emperor Reigen]] was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in Kansei3 ([[:en:1791|1791]]) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence, after the old building was destroyed in the Great Tenmei Fire of Tenmei8 ([[:en:1788|1788]]). In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was categorized as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fu-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Notably, scholar {{ill|Shiga Tsuyoshi|ja|志賀剛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Tsuyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Shikinaisha has identified this shrine with {{ill|Izumo Iokami Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Iokami Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shikinai Taisha) as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The enshrined deities consist of the following eight gods, collectively known as the "Eight Goryo": * Kibi Seirei (although often identified with [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi Mabibi]], since Kibi Mabibi did not die in a state of indignation, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|Japanese spirit|ja|和魂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese spirit|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}} of the six divine spirits) * Emperor Suidō (the crown prince of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]], [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Hayara]]) * {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}} (son of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]]) * Lady Fujiwara (mother of Prince Iyo, {{ill|Fujiwara Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Yoshiko|1=Fujiwara no Yoshiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623103}}) * Fujiwara Daitō ({{ill|Fujiwara Hirotsugu|ja|藤原広嗣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hirotsugu|1=Fujiwara no Hirotsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090626}}) * Tachibana Daifu ([[:en:Tachibana no Hayanari|Tachibana Itsusei]]) * Fumi Daifu ({{ill|Fumimuro no Miyata no Maro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|lt=Fumi Daifu|lt_ja=文屋宮田麻呂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fumimuro no Miyata no Maro|1=Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499944}}) * Karei Tenjin (although often identified with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, since the shrine was founded before he came to be regarded as Tenjin, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|rough spirit|ja|荒魂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rough spirit|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}} of the six divine spirits) All these deities are individuals who met their untimely deaths amid political strife, and this shrine was established to appease their vengeful spirits ([[:en:Onryō|Vengeful Spirit]]). In the side hall, [[:en:Emperor Reigen|Emperor Reigen]] is enshrined as the Tennchūbashira Imperial Deity, which is attributed to the close ties between the shrine’s then priests, the father and son {{ill|Izumori Nobu-nao|ja|出雲路信直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumori Nobu-nao|1=Izumoji Nobunao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395908}} and {{ill|Izumori Naomoto|ja|出雲路直元|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumori Naomoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the emperor. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) – Reconstructed in Kansei3 ([[:en:1791|1791]]) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence. * Offerings Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Corridor (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Worship Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Prayer Hall * Shrine Office * Shinmeisha ({{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|lt=Naiku|lt_ja=内宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and {{ill|Kōtai Shrine|ja|大将軍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai-Shogun Shrine|1=taishōgun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5369529}} Shrine, Kochiho Shrine, {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 愛宕権現}}, and {{ill|Hiyoshisha|ja|日吉社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiyoshisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日吉大社|1=Hiyoshi Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q656451}} * Kagura Hall * Palanquin Pavilion * Shrine Gate == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * [[:en:Emperor Reigen|Emperor Reigen]]’s Imperial Edict Prayer Document === Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto City === * Main Hall * Offerings Hall * Worship Hall * Corridor == Festivals == Until the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] the Mikoshi Reception was held on {{ill|July 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月18日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 18 (lunar calendar)|1=18th day of the 7th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839924}} and the Goryo Festival on {{ill|August 18 (Lunisolar)|ja|8月18日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 18 (lunar calendar)|qq=}}. After the Meiji period, May1 was designated as the Divine Auspice Festival (Shinkō‑sai) and the 18th as the Homecoming Festival (Kankō‑sai), with August18 established as the regular festival day on which a Tōyū performance is offered. Currently, the Homecoming Festival is held on either the third or fourth Sunday of May, and during the evening festival a children’s mikoshi is organized in collaboration with local elementary schools. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Goryō|Goryo Faith]] * {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Goryo Shrine|1=Kamigoryō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359018}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimogoryo-jinja}} * [http://shimogoryo.main.jp/ Shimogoryo Shrine Official Website] {{Kyōyō Tenmangu Twenty-Five Shrine Pilgrimage}} {{Shinto Portal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|下御霊神社|version=101246179|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] [[Category:Emperor Reigen]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:下御霊神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shimo-Goryō Shrine |location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto]], Nakagyō Ward, Teramachi-dōri Marutamachi-sagaru Shimogoryōmae-chō |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|01|01|N|135|46|04|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shimo-Goryō Shrine}} |deity = Yoshitsugu Seiō

Kazan Tenjin |consecration_year = [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] 5 (863 CE) |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Festival = 3rd Sunday of May (Goryō Festival) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Shimogoryo_torii.jpg|250px|]] }} '''Shimo-Goryō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|下御霊神社}}, Shimogoryō Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|Nakagyō-ku]], Kyoto City. Its name corresponds to {{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}, reflecting its complementary role. The shrine is historically regarded as one of the key sites of [[:en:Goryō|goryō beliefs]], dedicated to appeasing vengeful spirits. == History == The shrine traces its origins to the [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 5 (863 CE) {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|1=goryō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489354}}, a ritual held at the [[:en:Shinsenen|Shinsen-en]] in Kyoto to appease the spirits of those who had died tragically. The epidemic plaguing the land at the time was believed to be caused by these restless spirits. Shimo-Goryō Shrine and its counterpart Kamigoryō Shrine were both established following this ceremony. Originally, Shimo-Goryō Shrine was located in Izumojizō in the {{ill|Atago District|ja|愛宕郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago District|1=Otagi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10981345}} but was moved to Shinmachi Demizu. In [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 18 (1590 CE), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] relocated the shrine to its current location, the former estate of [[:en:Saionji Saneuji|Saionji Sanemitsu]]. The shrine's main hall dates back to [[:en:Kansei|Kansei]] 3 (1791 CE), after the original structure was destroyed in the {{ill|Great Tenmei Fire|ja|天明の大火|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Tenmei Fire|1=Great Fire of Tenmei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442674}}. The current hall was repurposed from the temporary inner sanctum of the Imperial Palace. During the [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] era (1716–1736), [[:en:Emperor Reigen|Emperor Reigen]]'s spirit was enshrined as a secondary deity, reflecting the close ties between the shrine's priests and the emperor. == Deities == Shimo-Goryō Shrine enshrines eight primary deities, collectively known as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Yoshitsugu Seiō (interpreted as the harmonious spirit of the six deities) * {{ill|Sudō Tennō|ja|崇道天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 早良親王|1=Prince Sawara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1079634}} (Prince Sawara, son of Emperor Kanmu) * {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}} (son of Emperor Kanmu) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|1=Fujiwara no Yoshiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623103}} (mother of Prince Iyo) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Hirotsugu|ja|藤原広嗣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Hirotsugu|1=Fujiwara no Hirotsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090626}} * [[:en:Tachibana no Hayanari|Tachibana no Hayanari]] * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|1=Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499944}} * Kazan Tenjin (interpreted as the wrathful spirit of the six deities) Additionally, Emperor Reigen is enshrined in the main hall's auxiliary sanctum. These deities represent figures who suffered tragic or unjust deaths, and the shrine was founded to appease their spirits and prevent their wrath from causing harm. == Cultural Assets == === Important Cultural Property === * A prayer document written by [[:en:Emperor Reigen|Emperor Reigen]] === Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Main hall * Hei-den (offering hall) * Covered corridors * Worship hall == Festivals == The shrine's annual {{ill|Goryō Festival|ja|御霊祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is one of its most significant events. Traditionally held on [[:en:July 18|July 18 (old calendar)]] and [[:en:August 18|August 18 (old calendar)]], the dates were adjusted in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] to May 1 for the Shinkōsai (portable shrine procession) and May 18 for the Kangyōsai (return procession). The modern festival is celebrated on the third or fourth Sunday of May, with events such as children's mikoshi parades organized in collaboration with local schools. == External Links == * [http://shimogoryo.main.jp/ Official Website] * {{Commonscat|Shimo-Goryō Shrine}} {{洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝}} {{translated page|ja|下御霊神社|version=101246179|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:下御霊神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Important Scenic Structures in Kyōto Prefecture]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of 25 Shrines of Rakuyō Tenmangū]] [[Category:Emperor Reigen]] {{Commons category|Shimogoryō-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11360904}}

火雷天神 | founded = {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|Jōgan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}5th year （[[:en:863|863]]） | status = 旧{{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fu-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | pilgrimage = {{ill|Kyoto-Rakuya Hachasha Shūin Meguri|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto-Rakuya Hachasha Shūin Meguri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | festival = 5月第3日曜日（御霊祭） | File = [[File:Shimogoryo_torii.jpg|250px|]] | image = File:Shimogoryo torii.jpg | location = {{ill|Nakagyō Ward|WD=Q1075455}} | deity = {{ill|Fujiwara no Hirotsugu|WD=Q3090626}}; {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|WD=Q11623103}}; {{ill|Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|WD=Q11499944}}; {{ill|Iyo-shinnō|WD=Q11378831}}; {{ill|Kibi-naishinnō|WD=Q11412639}}; {{ill|Prince Sawara|WD=Q1079634}}; {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|WD=Q1062686}} }} '''Shimogoryo Shrine''' (下御霊神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]][[:en:Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|Nakagyo Ward]], Shimogoryo‑mae Town. It was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fu-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine’s name corresponds with that of {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Goryo Shrine|1=Kamigoryō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359018}}. == History == During the era of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]], epidemics spread throughout various regions. This was regarded as a curse ({{ill|Curse|ja|祟り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Curse|1=tatari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109944776}}) stemming from the [[:en:Goryō|Spirits]], and accordingly, on May 20 of {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|Jōgan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}5 ([[:en:863|863]]), a Goryo Gathering ({{ill|Goryo Gathering|ja|御霊会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryo Gathering|1=goryō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489354}}) was held at the [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]] site of [[:en:Shinsenen|Shinsen-en]]. This gathering is considered to mark the founding of the enshrinements at both this shrine and {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Goryo Shrine|1=Kamigoryō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359018}}. Since ancient times, it has been revered as the tutelary deity of the [[:en:Kyoto Imperial Palace|Kyoto Imperial Palace]]. Originally, the deity was enshrined in the Goryo Hall of Shimodashimori‑ji (下出雲寺御霊堂) located in the Izumo District of Atago County ({{ill|Atago District|ja|愛宕郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago District|1=Otagi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10981345}}, {{ill|Izumo District|ja|出雲郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) on the route of {{ill|Izumo Road|ja|出雲路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Road|1=Izumoji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395906}}. To the north stood {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Goryo Shrine|1=Kamigoryō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359018}}, known as the Goryo Hall of Kamidashimori‑ji. Later, the shrine was relocated to Shinmachi Izumi, and subsequently, in Tenshō18 ([[:en:1590|1590]]), as part of the urban development initiated by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], it was moved to the site where [[:en:Saionji Saneuji|Saionji Sanjitsu]] had his villa, Tokiwaido. During the Kyōhō era ([[:en:1716|1716]] – [[:en:1736|1736]]), the spirit of [[:en:Emperor Reigen|Emperor Reigen]] was also enshrined. The current shrine building was reconstructed in Kansei3 ([[:en:1791|1791]]) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence, after the old building was destroyed in the Great Tenmei Fire of Tenmei8 ([[:en:1788|1788]]). In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was categorized as a {{ill|Fu-sha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fu-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Notably, scholar {{ill|Shiga Tsuyoshi|ja|志賀剛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Tsuyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Shikinaisha has identified this shrine with {{ill|Izumo Iokami Shrine|ja|出雲井於神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Iokami Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shikinai Taisha) as recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} The enshrined deities consist of the following eight gods, collectively known as the "Eight Goryo": * Kibi Seirei (although often identified with [[:en:Kibi no Makibi|Kibi Mabibi]], since Kibi Mabibi did not die in a state of indignation, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|Japanese spirit|ja|和魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese spirit|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}} of the six divine spirits) * Emperor Suidō (the crown prince of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]], [[:en:Prince Sawara|Prince Hayara]]) * {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|qq=}} (son of [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Emperor Kanmu]]) * Lady Fujiwara (mother of Prince Iyo, {{ill|Fujiwara Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Yoshiko|1=Fujiwara no Yoshiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623103}}) * Fujiwara Daitō ({{ill|Fujiwara Hirotsugu|ja|藤原広嗣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hirotsugu|1=Fujiwara no Hirotsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090626}}) * Tachibana Daifu ([[:en:Tachibana no Hayanari|Tachibana Itsusei]]) * Fumi Daifu ({{ill|Fumimuro no Miyata no Maro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|lt=Fumi Daifu|lt_ja=文屋宮田麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fumimuro no Miyata no Maro|1=Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499944}}) * Karei Tenjin (although often identified with {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, since the shrine was founded before he came to be regarded as Tenjin, the shrine interprets this deity as the {{ill|rough spirit|ja|荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rough spirit|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q495568}} of the six divine spirits) All these deities are individuals who met their untimely deaths amid political strife, and this shrine was established to appease their vengeful spirits ([[:en:Onryō|Vengeful Spirit]]). In the side hall, [[:en:Emperor Reigen|Emperor Reigen]] is enshrined as the Tennchūbashira Imperial Deity, which is attributed to the close ties between the shrine’s then priests, the father and son {{ill|Izumori Nobu-nao|ja|出雲路信直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumori Nobu-nao|1=Izumoji Nobunao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395908}} and {{ill|Izumori Naomoto|ja|出雲路直元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumori Naomoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the emperor. == Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) – Reconstructed in Kansei3 ([[:en:1791|1791]]) by relocating the provisional palace of the Naishi‑dokoro of the temporary imperial residence. * Offerings Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Corridor (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Worship Hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Kyoto City) * Prayer Hall * Shrine Office * Shinmeisha ({{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|lt=Naiku|lt_ja=内宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊受大神宮}}) * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日大社}} – Built during the [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei era]] ([[:en:1818|1818]] – [[:en:1831|1831]]) * Sarutahiko Shrine, Tarika Shrine, and Kakimoto Shrine – Enshrining, in addition to {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, [[:en:Yamazaki Ansai|Yamazaki Ansai]], who advocated {{ill|Tarika Shintō|ja|垂加神道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarika Shintō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Suika Shinto|WD=Q11425803}}, and [[:en:Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|Kakimoto no Hitomaro]] * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神社|1=Inari shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q514480}} * {{ill|Munakata Shrine|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Munakata Shrine|lt_ja=宗像社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} * [[:en:Tenmangū|Choraku-ji Tenmangū]] – Enshrined deity: Kitano Ōkami * [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi Shrine]] * Goshasōdensho – Saibe Shrine, {{ill|Dai-Shogun Shrine|ja|大将軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai-Shogun Shrine|1=taishōgun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5369529}} Shrine, Kochiho Shrine, {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 愛宕権現}}, and {{ill|Hiyoshisha|ja|日吉社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiyoshisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日吉大社|1=Hiyoshi Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q656451}} * Kagura Hall * Palanquin Pavilion * Shrine Gate == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * [[:en:Emperor Reigen|Emperor Reigen]]’s Imperial Edict Prayer Document === Designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Kyoto City === * Main Hall * Offerings Hall * Worship Hall * Corridor == Festivals == Until the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] the Mikoshi Reception was held on {{ill|July 18 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月18日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 18 (lunar calendar)|1=18th day of the 7th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839924}} and the Goryo Festival on {{ill|August 18 (Lunisolar)|ja|8月18日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 18 (lunar calendar)|qq=}}. After the Meiji period, May1 was designated as the Divine Auspice Festival (Shinkō‑sai) and the 18th as the Homecoming Festival (Kankō‑sai), with August18 established as the regular festival day on which a Tōyū performance is offered. Currently, the Homecoming Festival is held on either the third or fourth Sunday of May, and during the evening festival a children’s mikoshi is organized in collaboration with local elementary schools. == References == {{Reference Help}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Goryō|Goryo Faith]] * {{ill|Kami-Goryo Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Goryo Shrine|1=Kamigoryō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359018}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shimogoryo-jinja}} * [http://shimogoryo.main.jp/ Shimogoryo Shrine Official Website] {{Kyōyō Tenmangu Twenty-Five Shrine Pilgrimage}} {{Shinto Portal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|下御霊神社|version=101246179|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] [[Category:Emperor Reigen]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:下御霊神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shimo-Goryō Shrine |location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto]], Nakagyō Ward, Teramachi-dōri Marutamachi-sagaru Shimogoryōmae-chō |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|01|01|N|135|46|04|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shimo-Goryō Shrine}} |deity = Yoshitsugu Seiō
[[:en:Prince Sawara|Sudō Tennō]]
[[Iyo Shinnō|Prince Iyo]]
[[:en:Fujiwara no Yoshiko|Fujiwara Daibujin]]
[[Fujiwara no Hirotsugu|Fuji Daibu]]
[[:en:Tachibana no Hayanari|Tachibana Daibu]]
[[Funya no Miyatamaro|Funya Daibu]]
Kazan Tenjin |consecration_year = [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] 5 (863 CE) |Shrine Rank = Former {{ill|Fusha|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja|京洛八社集印めぐり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto's Eight Shrines Collection Tour|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Festival = 3rd Sunday of May (Goryō Festival) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Shimogoryo_torii.jpg|250px|]] }} '''Shimo-Goryō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|下御霊神社}}, Shimogoryō Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|Nakagyō-ku]], Kyoto City. Its name corresponds to {{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}}, reflecting its complementary role. The shrine is historically regarded as one of the key sites of [[:en:Goryō|goryō beliefs]], dedicated to appeasing vengeful spirits. == History == The shrine traces its origins to the [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 5 (863 CE) {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|1=goryō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489354}}, a ritual held at the [[:en:Shinsenen|Shinsen-en]] in Kyoto to appease the spirits of those who had died tragically. The epidemic plaguing the land at the time was believed to be caused by these restless spirits. Shimo-Goryō Shrine and its counterpart Kamigoryō Shrine were both established following this ceremony. Originally, Shimo-Goryō Shrine was located in Izumojizō in the {{ill|Atago District|ja|愛宕郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago District|1=Otagi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10981345}} but was moved to Shinmachi Demizu. In [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 18 (1590 CE), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] relocated the shrine to its current location, the former estate of [[:en:Saionji Saneuji|Saionji Sanemitsu]]. The shrine's main hall dates back to [[:en:Kansei|Kansei]] 3 (1791 CE), after the original structure was destroyed in the {{ill|Great Tenmei Fire|ja|天明の大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Tenmei Fire|1=Great Fire of Tenmei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442674}}. The current hall was repurposed from the temporary inner sanctum of the Imperial Palace. During the [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] era (1716–1736), [[:en:Emperor Reigen|Emperor Reigen]]'s spirit was enshrined as a secondary deity, reflecting the close ties between the shrine's priests and the emperor. == Deities == Shimo-Goryō Shrine enshrines eight primary deities, collectively known as the "Eight Goryō Deities" (八所御霊): * Yoshitsugu Seiō (interpreted as the harmonious spirit of the six deities) * {{ill|Sudō Tennō|ja|崇道天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudō Tennō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 早良親王|1=Prince Sawara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1079634}} (Prince Sawara, son of Emperor Kanmu) * {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|lt=Iyo Shinnō|lt=Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}} (son of Emperor Kanmu) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|1=Fujiwara no Yoshiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623103}} (mother of Prince Iyo) * {{ill|Fujiwara no Hirotsugu|ja|藤原広嗣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Hirotsugu|1=Fujiwara no Hirotsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090626}} * [[:en:Tachibana no Hayanari|Tachibana no Hayanari]] * {{ill|Funya no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funya no Miyatamaro|1=Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499944}} * Kazan Tenjin (interpreted as the wrathful spirit of the six deities) Additionally, Emperor Reigen is enshrined in the main hall's auxiliary sanctum. These deities represent figures who suffered tragic or unjust deaths, and the shrine was founded to appease their spirits and prevent their wrath from causing harm. == Cultural Assets == === Important Cultural Property === * A prayer document written by [[:en:Emperor Reigen|Emperor Reigen]] === Kyoto City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Main hall * Hei-den (offering hall) * Covered corridors * Worship hall == Festivals == The shrine's annual {{ill|Goryō Festival|ja|御霊祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is one of its most significant events. Traditionally held on [[:en:July 18|July 18 (old calendar)]] and [[:en:August 18|August 18 (old calendar)]], the dates were adjusted in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] to May 1 for the Shinkōsai (portable shrine procession) and May 18 for the Kangyōsai (return procession). The modern festival is celebrated on the third or fourth Sunday of May, with events such as children's mikoshi parades organized in collaboration with local schools. == External Links == * [http://shimogoryo.main.jp/ Official Website] * {{Commonscat|Shimo-Goryō Shrine}} {{洛陽天満宮二十五社順拝}} {{translated page|ja|下御霊神社|version=101246179|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:下御霊神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Important Scenic Structures in Kyōto Prefecture]] [[Category:Pilgrimage of 25 Shrines of Rakuyō Tenmangū]] [[Category:Emperor Reigen]] {{Commons category|Shimogoryō-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11360904}}
